Benchmark Results for claude-opus-4-5

Test 1: Travelling Salesman Problem Solver (C++) Score: 5.00 / 21
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP) in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY number of cities from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 10-40 cities (brute-force feasible for smallest cases)
- **Medium**: 100-1000 cities (requires heuristics)
- **Large**: 5000-100000 cities (requires efficient heuristics)
- **Extreme**: 500000-5000000 cities (requires very fast approximations)
- **Epic**: 10000000+ cities (requires highly optimized approximations)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with city counts ranging from 10 to 10000000. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N (number of cities)
Lines 2..N+1: x y coordinates (floats)

**Output format (stdout):**
One line with N integers: the visit order of city indices (0-indexed), starting at city 0.
Do NOT include returning to city 0 (return is implicit).

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 300 seconds even for 1000,000,000 cities
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable routes at all scales
4. **Correctness**: Output must be a valid permutation of 0..N-1 starting with 0

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function that reads stdin and writes stdout.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on N.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: 10 Cities
View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a list of cities, find the shortest round-trip route that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. This is one of the most famous hard problems in computer science — checking every possible route is only feasible for a handful of cities, so the AI must use clever shortcuts to find a good route quickly.

Subpasses scale from 10 cities up to 1 000, so brute-force is impossible for the larger cases. The AI's route length is compared against a simple baseline; beating it earns full marks.

 1.0000
[10 cities] Route distance: 2449.07, Baseline: 2922.93, Ratio: 0.84x, Time: 0.7s - excellent (within 10% of baseline)

TSP Solver - 10 Cities

View Code (7812 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <random>
#include <chrono>
#include <limits>
#include <unordered_map>

using namespace std;

struct Point {
    float x, y;
    int idx;
};

inline float dist(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    float dx = a.x - b.x;
    float dy = a.y - b.y;
    return sqrtf(dx*dx + dy*dy);
}

inline float distSq(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    float dx = a.x - b.x;
    float dy = a.y - b.y;
    return dx*dx + dy*dy;
}

vector<int> nearestNeighbor(const vector<Point>& cities) {
    int n = (int)cities.size();
    vector<int> tour;
    tour.reserve(n);
    vector<bool> visited(n, false);

    int current = 0;
    tour.push_back(current);
    visited[current] = true;

    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
        float bestDist = numeric_limits<float>::max();
        int bestNext = -1;
        for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) {
            if (!visited[j]) {
                float d = distSq(cities[current], cities[j]);
                if (d < bestDist) {
                    bestDist = d;
                    bestNext = j;
                }
            }
        }
        tour.push_back(bestNext);
        visited[bestNext] = true;
        current = bestNext;
    }
    return tour;
}

void twoOptImprove(vector<int>& tour, const vector<Point>& cities, int maxIter = 1000000) {
    int n = (int)tour.size();
    if (n < 4) return;

    bool improved = true;
    int iter = 0;
    while (improved && iter < maxIter) {
        improved = false;
        iter++;
        for (int i = 0; i < n - 2 && !improved; i++) {
            for (int j = i + 2; j < n; j++) {
                if (i == 0 && j == n - 1) continue;

                int i1 = tour[i], i2 = tour[i+1];
                int j1 = tour[j], j2 = tour[(j+1) % n];

                float d1 = dist(cities[i1], cities[i2]) + dist(cities[j1], cities[j2]);
                float d2 = dist(cities[i1], cities[j1]) + dist(cities[i2], cities[j2]);

                if (d2 < d1 - 1e-6f) {
                    reverse(tour.begin() + i + 1, tour.begin() + j + 1);
                    improved = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

void limitedTwoOpt(vector<int>& tour, const vector<Point>& cities, int passes) {
    int n = (int)tour.size();
    if (n < 4) return;

    for (int pass = 0; pass < passes; pass++) {
        bool improved = false;
        for (int i = 0; i < n - 2; i++) {
            int jEnd = min(i + 50, n);
            for (int j = i + 2; j < jEnd; j++) {
                if (i == 0 && j == n - 1) continue;

                int i1 = tour[i], i2 = tour[i+1];
                int j1 = tour[j], j2 = tour[(j+1) % n];

                float d1 = dist(cities[i1], cities[i2]) + dist(cities[j1], cities[j2]);
                float d2 = dist(cities[i1], cities[j1]) + dist(cities[i2], cities[j2]);

                if (d2 < d1 - 1e-6f) {
                    reverse(tour.begin() + i + 1, tour.begin() + j + 1);
                    improved = true;
                }
            }
        }
        if (!improved) break;
    }
}

struct GridCell {
    vector<int> points;
};

vector<int> gridBasedTour(vector<Point>& cities) {
    int n = (int)cities.size();

    float minX = cities[0].x, maxX = cities[0].x;
    float minY = cities[0].y, maxY = cities[0].y;
    for (const auto& c : cities) {
        minX = min(minX, c.x); maxX = max(maxX, c.x);
        minY = min(minY, c.y); maxY = max(maxY, c.y);
    }

    int gridSize = max(1, (int)sqrt((double)n / 10.0));
    float cellW = (maxX - minX + 1e-6f) / gridSize;
    float cellH = (maxY - minY + 1e-6f) / gridSize;

    vector<vector<GridCell>> grid(gridSize, vector<GridCell>(gridSize));

    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        int gx = min(gridSize - 1, (int)((cities[i].x - minX) / cellW));
        int gy = min(gridSize - 1, (int)((cities[i].y - minY) / cellH));
        grid[gx][gy].points.push_back(i);
    }

    for (int gx = 0; gx < gridSize; gx++) {
        for (int gy = 0; gy < gridSize; gy++) {
            auto& pts = grid[gx][gy].points;
            if (pts.size() > 1) {
                sort(pts.begin(), pts.end(), [&](int a, int b) {
                    return cities[a].x + cities[a].y < cities[b].x + cities[b].y;
                });
            }
        }
    }

    vector<int> tour;
    tour.reserve(n);
    vector<bool> visitedCell(gridSize * gridSize, false);

    int startGx = min(gridSize - 1, (int)((cities[0].x - minX) / cellW));
    int startGy = min(gridSize - 1, (int)((cities[0].y - minY) / cellH));

    auto addCell = [&](int gx, int gy, bool reverse_order) {
        auto& pts = grid[gx][gy].points;
        if (reverse_order) {
            for (int i = (int)pts.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
                tour.push_back(pts[i]);
            }
        } else {
            for (int p : pts) tour.push_back(p);
        }
    };

    bool leftToRight = true;
    for (int gy = 0; gy < gridSize; gy++) {
        if (leftToRight) {
            for (int gx = 0; gx < gridSize; gx++) {
                addCell(gx, gy, false);
            }
        } else {
            for (int gx = gridSize - 1; gx >= 0; gx--) {
                addCell(gx, gy, true);
            }
        }
        leftToRight = !leftToRight;
    }

    int startPos = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < (int)tour.size(); i++) {
        if (tour[i] == 0) { startPos = i; break; }
    }

    vector<int> result;
    result.reserve(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        result.push_back(tour[(startPos + i) % n]);
    }

    return result;
}

vector<int> stripeTour(vector<Point>& cities) {
    int n = (int)cities.size();

    vector<int> indices(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) indices[i] = i;

    float minX = cities[0].x, maxX = cities[0].x;
    for (const auto& c : cities) {
        minX = min(minX, c.x); maxX = max(maxX, c.x);
    }

    int numStripes = max(1, (int)sqrt((double)n / 100.0));
    float stripeW = (maxX - minX + 1e-6f) / numStripes;

    sort(indices.begin(), indices.end(), [&](int a, int b) {
        int sa = min(numStripes - 1, (int)((cities[a].x - minX) / stripeW));
        int sb = min(numStripes - 1, (int)((cities[b].x - minX) / stripeW));
        if (sa != sb) return sa < sb;
        if (sa % 2 == 0) return cities[a].y < cities[b].y;
        return cities[a].y > cities[b].y;
    });

    int startPos = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (indices[i] == 0) { startPos = i; break; }
    }

    vector<int> result;
    result.reserve(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        result.push_back(indices[(startPos + i) % n]);
    }

    return result;
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n;
    cin >> n;

    vector<Point> cities(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        cin >> cities[i].x >> cities[i].y;
        cities[i].idx = i;
    }

    vector<int> tour;

    if (n <= 1) {
        tour.push_back(0);
    } else if (n <= 2000) {
        tour = nearestNeighbor(cities);
        twoOptImprove(tour, cities, n * n);
    } else if (n <= 20000) {
        tour = nearestNeighbor(cities);
        limitedTwoOpt(tour, cities, 5);
    } else if (n <= 200000) {
        tour = gridBasedTour(cities);
        limitedTwoOpt(tour, cities, 2);
    } else {
        tour = stripeTour(cities);
    }

    if (tour[0] != 0) {
        int startPos = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (tour[i] == 0) { startPos = i; break; }
        }
        vector<int> result;
        result.reserve(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            result.push_back(tour[(startPos + i) % n]);
        }
        tour = result;
    }

    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (i > 0) cout << ' ';
        cout << tour[i];
    }
    cout << '\n';

    return 0;
}

Test 2: Chinese Postman Problem Solver (C#) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Chinese Postman Problem (Route Inspection Problem) in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-20 nodes, 8-45 edges (small graphs, exact algorithms feasible)
- **Medium**: 35-200 nodes, 80-600 edges (requires efficient algorithms)
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, 2000-5000 edges (requires optimized implementations)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, 30000-80000 edges (requires very fast algorithms)
- **Epic**: 50000-100000 nodes, 500000-1000000 edges (requires highly optimized algorithms)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 100000 nodes. The same function must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v w (edge endpoints, 0-indexed, weight integer)

**Output format (stdout):**
A sequence of node indices (whitespace-separated) representing a route that starts at node 0,
traverses EVERY edge at least once, and returns to node 0.
You may optionally prefix with a single integer L (route length). If present, it must match the number of nodes listed.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Correctness**: Must traverse every edge at least once

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.

View chain of thought

Imagine a mail carrier who must walk along every street in a neighbourhood and return home. Some streets may need to be walked twice if the network doesn't allow a perfect loop — the goal is to minimise that extra walking.

The AI must figure out which streets (edges) to re-traverse and produce a complete route. Subpasses increase the graph size, making the problem harder to solve efficiently.

Chinese Postman Problem - 6 nodes, 8 edges

View Code (7003 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class ChinesePostman
{
    static int N, M;
    static List<(int to, int weight, int idx)>[] adj;
    static List<(int u, int v, int w)> edges;
    static int[] edgeCount;

    static void Main()
    {
        ReadInput();

        var oddVerts = new List<int>();
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
            if (Degree(i) % 2 == 1)
                oddVerts.Add(i);

        if (oddVerts.Count > 0)
        {
            var dist = new long[oddVerts.Count][];
            var paths = new List<int>[oddVerts.Count][];

            for (int i = 0; i < oddVerts.Count; i++)
            {
                dist[i] = new long[oddVerts.Count];
                paths[i] = new List<int>[oddVerts.Count];
                var (d, p) = Dijkstra(oddVerts[i], oddVerts);
                for (int j = 0; j < oddVerts.Count; j++)
                {
                    dist[i][j] = d[j];
                    paths[i][j] = p[j];
                }
            }

            var matching = MinMatching(dist, oddVerts.Count);

            foreach (var (i, j) in matching)
            {
                var path = paths[i][j];
                for (int k = 0; k < path.Count - 1; k++)
                {
                    int u = path[k], v = path[k + 1];
                    int minW = int.MaxValue;
                    foreach (var e in adj[u])
                        if (e.to == v && edges[e.idx].w < minW)
                            minW = edges[e.idx].w;
                    int idx = edges.Count;
                    edges.Add((u, v, minW));
                    adj[u].Add((v, minW, idx));
                    adj[v].Add((u, minW, idx));
                    edgeCount[idx] = 1;
                }
            }
        }

        var circuit = Hierholzer(0);
        Console.WriteLine(string.Join(" ", circuit));
    }

    static void ReadInput()
    {
        var line = Console.ReadLine().Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        N = int.Parse(line[0]);
        M = int.Parse(line[1]);

        adj = new List<(int, int, int)>[N];
        edges = new List<(int, int, int)>(M);
        edgeCount = new int[M + N * 2];

        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
            adj[i] = new List<(int, int, int)>();

        for (int i = 0; i < M; i++)
        {
            line = Console.ReadLine().Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
            int u = int.Parse(line[0]);
            int v = int.Parse(line[1]);
            int w = int.Parse(line[2]);
            edges.Add((u, v, w));
            adj[u].Add((v, w, i));
            adj[v].Add((u, w, i));
            edgeCount[i] = 1;
        }
    }

    static int Degree(int v)
    {
        int d = 0;
        foreach (var e in adj[v])
            d += edgeCount[e.idx];
        return d;
    }

    static (long[], List<int>[]) Dijkstra(int src, List<int> targets)
    {
        var targetSet = new HashSet<int>(targets);
        var dist = new long[N];
        var prev = new int[N];
        Array.Fill(dist, long.MaxValue);
        Array.Fill(prev, -1);
        dist[src] = 0;

        var pq = new SortedSet<(long d, int v)> { (0, src) };

        while (pq.Count > 0)
        {
            var (d, u) = pq.Min;
            pq.Remove(pq.Min);
            if (d > dist[u]) continue;

            foreach (var (v, w, _) in adj[u])
            {
                if (dist[u] + w < dist[v])
                {
                    pq.Remove((dist[v], v));
                    dist[v] = dist[u] + w;
                    prev[v] = u;
                    pq.Add((dist[v], v));
                }
            }
        }

        var result = new long[targets.Count];
        var paths = new List<int>[targets.Count];

        for (int i = 0; i < targets.Count; i++)
        {
            result[i] = dist[targets[i]];
            paths[i] = new List<int>();
            for (int v = targets[i]; v != -1; v = prev[v])
                paths[i].Add(v);
            paths[i].Reverse();
        }

        return (result, paths);
    }

    static List<(int, int)> MinMatching(long[][] dist, int n)
    {
        if (n <= 20)
            return DPMatching(dist, n);
        return GreedyMatching(dist, n);
    }

    static List<(int, int)> DPMatching(long[][] dist, int n)
    {
        int full = (1 << n) - 1;
        var dp = new long[1 << n];
        var parent = new int[1 << n];
        Array.Fill(dp, long.MaxValue);
        dp[0] = 0;

        for (int mask = 0; mask < (1 << n); mask++)
        {
            if (dp[mask] == long.MaxValue) continue;
            int first = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
                if ((mask & (1 << i)) == 0) { first = i; break; }
            if (first == -1) continue;

            for (int second = first + 1; second < n; second++)
            {
                if ((mask & (1 << second)) != 0) continue;
                int newMask = mask | (1 << first) | (1 << second);
                long newCost = dp[mask] + dist[first][second];
                if (newCost < dp[newMask])
                {
                    dp[newMask] = newCost;
                    parent[newMask] = mask | (first << 10) | (second << 20);
                }
            }
        }

        var result = new List<(int, int)>();
        int cur = full;
        while (cur != 0)
        {
            int p = parent[cur];
            int first = (p >> 10) & 0x3FF;
            int second = (p >> 20) & 0x3FF;
            result.Add((first, second));
            cur = p & 0x3FF;
        }
        return result;
    }

    static List<(int, int)> GreedyMatching(long[][] dist, int n)
    {
        var used = new bool[n];
        var result = new List<(int, int)>();
        var candidates = new List<(long d, int i, int j)>();

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            for (int j = i + 1; j < n; j++)
                candidates.Add((dist[i][j], i, j));

        candidates.Sort();

        foreach (var (_, i, j) in candidates)
        {
            if (used[i] || used[j]) continue;
            used[i] = used[j] = true;
            result.Add((i, j));
        }
        return result;
    }

    static List<int> Hierholzer(int start)
    {
        var localCount = new int[edgeCount.Length];
        Array.Copy(edgeCount, localCount, edgeCount.Length);

        var stack = new Stack<int>();
        var circuit = new List<int>();
        stack.Push(start);

        while (stack.Count > 0)
        {
            int u = stack.Peek();
            bool found = false;

            foreach (var (v, _, idx) in adj[u])
            {
                if (localCount[idx] > 0)
                {
                    localCount[idx]--;
                    stack.Push(v);
                    found = true;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if (!found)
                circuit.Add(stack.Pop());
        }

        circuit.Reverse();
        return circuit;
    }
}

Test 3: Graph Layout - Edge Crossing Minimization (Rust) Score: 4.00 / 16
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Graph Layout problem (Edge Crossing Minimization) in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-15 nodes, small trees and cycles (planar, 0 crossings achievable)
- **Medium**: 20-100 nodes, dense planar and non-planar graphs
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, random dense graphs (many crossings unavoidable)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, very large random graphs

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 200,000 nodes. The same solver must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v (undirected edge endpoints, 0-indexed)

**Output format (stdout):**
Output N lines, one per node, with: x y coordinates (floats).
You may optionally prefix with a single integer N on the first line.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable layouts with minimal crossings
4. **Robustness**: Handle disconnected components, self-loops, and parallel edges gracefully

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3

**Rust Compiler:**

View chain of thought

Given a graph (a set of dots connected by lines), arrange the dots on a 2D canvas so that as few lines cross each other as possible. A clean, readable layout is the goal — tangled spaghetti is the enemy.

Minimising crossings is extremely hard in general. The AI must place nodes intelligently; the baseline simply puts them in a circle. Subpasses increase the number of nodes and edges.

 1.0000
[tree, 6 nodes, 5 edges] Crossings: 0, Baseline: 1, Time: 0.1s - perfect (0 crossings)

Graph Layout - tree (6 nodes, 5 edges)

View Code (5867 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write, BufWriter};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());

    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();

    let first_line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
    let mut parts = first_line.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let m: usize = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    if n == 0 {
        return;
    }

    let mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for _ in 0..m {
        if let Some(Ok(line)) = lines.next() {
            let mut parts = line.split_whitespace();
            let u: usize = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
            let v: usize = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
            if u != v && u < n && v < n {
                adj[u].push(v);
                adj[v].push(u);
            }
        }
    }

    for i in 0..n {
        adj[i].sort_unstable();
        adj[i].dedup();
    }

    let mut pos: Vec<(f64, f64)> = vec![(0.0, 0.0); n];
    let area = (n as f64).sqrt() * 100.0;
    let k = (area / (n as f64).sqrt()).max(1.0);

    let mut visited = vec![false; n];
    let mut component_start_x = 0.0;

    for start in 0..n {
        if visited[start] {
            continue;
        }

        let mut queue = VecDeque::new();
        let mut component = Vec::new();
        queue.push_back(start);
        visited[start] = true;

        while let Some(node) = queue.pop_front() {
            component.push(node);
            for &neighbor in &adj[node] {
                if !visited[neighbor] {
                    visited[neighbor] = true;
                    queue.push_back(neighbor);
                }
            }
        }

        let comp_size = component.len();
        let cols = (comp_size as f64).sqrt().ceil() as usize;

        for (idx, &node) in component.iter().enumerate() {
            let row = idx / cols;
            let col = idx % cols;
            let jitter_x = ((node * 7) % 100) as f64 / 500.0;
            let jitter_y = ((node * 13) % 100) as f64 / 500.0;
            pos[node] = (
                component_start_x + col as f64 * k + jitter_x,
                row as f64 * k + jitter_y,
            );
        }

        component_start_x += (cols as f64 + 2.0) * k;
    }

    let iterations = if n <= 50 {
        300
    } else if n <= 500 {
        150
    } else if n <= 5000 {
        80
    } else if n <= 50000 {
        30
    } else {
        15
    };

    let mut temp = area / 10.0;
    let cooling = temp / (iterations as f64 + 1.0);

    let use_grid = n > 500;

    for _ in 0..iterations {
        let mut disp: Vec<(f64, f64)> = vec![(0.0, 0.0); n];

        if use_grid {
            let cell_size = k * 2.0;
            let mut grid: std::collections::HashMap<(i64, i64), Vec<usize>> = std::collections::HashMap::new();

            for i in 0..n {
                let cx = (pos[i].0 / cell_size).floor() as i64;
                let cy = (pos[i].1 / cell_size).floor() as i64;
                grid.entry((cx, cy)).or_default().push(i);
            }

            for i in 0..n {
                let cx = (pos[i].0 / cell_size).floor() as i64;
                let cy = (pos[i].1 / cell_size).floor() as i64;

                for dx in -2i64..=2 {
                    for dy in -2i64..=2 {
                        if let Some(cell) = grid.get(&(cx + dx, cy + dy)) {
                            for &j in cell {
                                if i >= j {
                                    continue;
                                }
                                let (dx, dy) = (pos[i].0 - pos[j].0, pos[i].1 - pos[j].1);
                                let dist = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt().max(0.01);
                                let force = k * k / dist;
                                let fx = dx / dist * force;
                                let fy = dy / dist * force;
                                disp[i].0 += fx;
                                disp[i].1 += fy;
                                disp[j].0 -= fx;
                                disp[j].1 -= fy;
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        } else {
            for i in 0..n {
                for j in (i + 1)..n {
                    let (dx, dy) = (pos[i].0 - pos[j].0, pos[i].1 - pos[j].1);
                    let dist = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt().max(0.01);
                    let force = k * k / dist;
                    let fx = dx / dist * force;
                    let fy = dy / dist * force;
                    disp[i].0 += fx;
                    disp[i].1 += fy;
                    disp[j].0 -= fx;
                    disp[j].1 -= fy;
                }
            }
        }

        for u in 0..n {
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                if u < v {
                    let (dx, dy) = (pos[u].0 - pos[v].0, pos[u].1 - pos[v].1);
                    let dist = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt().max(0.01);
                    let force = dist * dist / k;
                    let fx = dx / dist * force;
                    let fy = dy / dist * force;
                    disp[u].0 -= fx;
                    disp[u].1 -= fy;
                    disp[v].0 += fx;
                    disp[v].1 += fy;
                }
            }
        }

        for i in 0..n {
            let (dx, dy) = disp[i];
            let dist = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt().max(0.01);
            let capped = dist.min(temp);
            pos[i].0 += dx / dist * capped;
            pos[i].1 += dy / dist * capped;
        }

        temp -= cooling;
        if temp <= 0.0 {
            break;
        }
    }

    for (x, y) in pos {
        writeln!(out, "{:.6} {:.6}", x, y).unwrap();
    }
}

Test 4: Regular Tetrahedron Packing in Polyhedra (C++) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Packing problem: Pack regular tetrahedrons into polyhedra (C++).

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY container size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: V edge_length
Lines 2..V+1: x y z (container vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: K (number of tetrahedrons placed)
Lines 2..K+1: cx cy cz rx ry rz (center + Euler angles in radians)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container volume and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for massive containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of tetrahedrons while ensuring no overlaps

**Key Geometry (edge length e=1.0):**
- Height: h = e * sqrt(2/3) ≈ 0.8165
- Circumradius: R = e * sqrt(3/8) ≈ 0.6124
- Inradius: r = e / sqrt(24) ≈ 0.2041

Architecture: AMD64

Test 5: Hamiltonian Path on Grid with Obstacles (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Hamiltonian Path problem on 2D grids with obstacles in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY grid size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: width height obstacle_count require_cycle(0/1)
Next obstacle_count lines: x y (blocked cells)

**Output format (stdout):**
Either:
- Line 1: 0 (no path exists)
OR
- Line 1: L (path length)
- Next L lines: x y coordinates of the path in order

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on grid size and obstacle density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 2500 cell grids
3. **Correctness**: Must find valid paths or correctly report impossibility

**Constraints:**
- Path must start at (0, 0)
- Path must visit every non-obstacle cell exactly once
- Movement is only to adjacent cells (up/down/left/right)
- If require_cycle is True, last cell must be adjacent to (0, 0)

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt

View chain of thought

Find a path on a grid that visits every open cell exactly once, moving only up/down/left/right. Some cells are blocked by obstacles. Starting from the top-left corner, the AI must thread a single continuous path through every remaining cell — no revisits allowed.

This is an NP-complete problem, so brute-force search blows up quickly. Subpasses increase the grid size and obstacle count. The baseline uses a simple depth-first search.

 0.0000
[4x4, 1 obstacles] Compilation failed:
error: expected identifier, found reserved keyword `gen`
--> C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpr72tzya1.rs:60:13

| ^^^ expected

Hamiltonian Path - 4x4 grid, 1 obstacles

View Code (6716 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write, BufWriter};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());

    let mut input = String::new();
    stdin.lock().read_line(&mut input).unwrap();
    let p: Vec<usize> = input.split_whitespace().map(|s| s.parse().unwrap()).collect();
    let (w, h, obs_count, cycle) = (p[0], p[1], p[2], p[3] == 1);

    let mut blocked = vec![vec![false; h]; w];
    for _ in 0..obs_count {
        input.clear();
        stdin.lock().read_line(&mut input).unwrap();
        let c: Vec<usize> = input.split_whitespace().map(|s| s.parse().unwrap()).collect();
        blocked[c[0]][c[1]] = true;
    }

    let n = w * h - obs_count;

    if n == 0 || blocked[0][0] {
        writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
        return;
    }

    if n == 1 {
        writeln!(out, "{}", if cycle { "0" } else { "1\n0 0" }).unwrap();
        return;
    }

    if !is_connected(&blocked, w, h) {
        writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
        return;
    }

    if cycle {
        let (b, wh) = count_colors(&blocked, w, h);
        if b != wh {
            writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
            return;
        }
        if !has_neighbor(0, 0, &blocked, w, h) {
            writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
            return;
        }
    }

    if obs_count == 0 {
        if let Some(path) = snake_pattern(w, h, cycle) {
            output(&mut out, &path);
            return;
        }
    }

    let mut visited = vec![vec![false; h]; w];
    let mut seen = vec![vec![0u32; h]; w];
    let mut gen = 0u32;
    visited[0][0] = true;
    let mut path = vec![(0usize, 0usize)];

    if search(&blocked, &mut visited, &mut seen, &mut gen, &mut path, w, h, n, cycle) {
        output(&mut out, &path);
    } else {
        writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
    }
}

fn output<W: Write>(out: &mut W, path: &[(usize, usize)]) {
    writeln!(out, "{}", path.len()).unwrap();
    for &(x, y) in path {
        writeln!(out, "{} {}", x, y).unwrap();
    }
}

fn is_connected(blocked: &[Vec<bool>], w: usize, h: usize) -> bool {
    let mut start = None;
    let mut total = 0;
    for x in 0..w {
        for y in 0..h {
            if !blocked[x][y] {
                total += 1;
                if start.is_none() { start = Some((x, y)); }
            }
        }
    }
    let (sx, sy) = match start { Some(s) => s, None => return true };
    let mut seen = vec![vec![false; h]; w];
    let mut queue = VecDeque::new();
    queue.push_back((sx, sy));
    seen[sx][sy] = true;
    let mut count = 1;
    while let Some((x, y)) = queue.pop_front() {
        for (nx, ny) in adj(x, y, w, h) {
            if !blocked[nx][ny] && !seen[nx][ny] {
                seen[nx][ny] = true;
                count += 1;
                queue.push_back((nx, ny));
            }
        }
    }
    count == total
}

fn count_colors(blocked: &[Vec<bool>], w: usize, h: usize) -> (usize, usize) {
    let (mut b, mut wh) = (0, 0);
    for x in 0..w {
        for y in 0..h {
            if !blocked[x][y] {
                if (x + y) % 2 == 0 { wh += 1; } else { b += 1; }
            }
        }
    }
    (b, wh)
}

fn has_neighbor(x: usize, y: usize, blocked: &[Vec<bool>], w: usize, h: usize) -> bool {
    adj(x, y, w, h).iter().any(|&(nx, ny)| !blocked[nx][ny])
}

fn adj(x: usize, y: usize, w: usize, h: usize) -> Vec<(usize, usize)> {
    let mut r = Vec::with_capacity(4);
    if x > 0 { r.push((x-1, y)); }
    if x+1 < w { r.push((x+1, y)); }
    if y > 0 { r.push((x, y-1)); }
    if y+1 < h { r.push((x, y+1)); }
    r
}

fn snake_pattern(w: usize, h: usize, cycle: bool) -> Option<Vec<(usize, usize)>> {
    let n = w * h;
    if cycle && n % 2 != 0 { return None; }
    let mut path = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    if h % 2 == 0 {
        for x in 0..w {
            if x % 2 == 0 { for y in 0..h { path.push((x, y)); } }
            else { for y in (0..h).rev() { path.push((x, y)); } }
        }
    } else if w % 2 == 0 {
        for y in 0..h {
            if y % 2 == 0 { for x in 0..w { path.push((x, y)); } }
            else { for x in (0..w).rev() { path.push((x, y)); } }
        }
    } else {
        if cycle { return None; }
        for x in 0..w {
            if x % 2 == 0 { for y in 0..h { path.push((x, y)); } }
            else { for y in (0..h).rev() { path.push((x, y)); } }
        }
    }
    if cycle {
        let (lx, ly) = *path.last().unwrap();
        if !((lx == 0 && ly == 1) || (lx == 1 && ly == 0)) { return None; }
    }
    Some(path)
}

fn search(
    blocked: &[Vec<bool>], visited: &mut [Vec<bool>], seen: &mut [Vec<u32>], gen: &mut u32,
    path: &mut Vec<(usize, usize)>, w: usize, h: usize, n: usize, cycle: bool
) -> bool {
    if path.len() == n {
        if !cycle { return true; }
        let (x, y) = *path.last().unwrap();
        return (x == 0 && y == 1) || (x == 1 && y == 0);
    }
    let (cx, cy) = *path.last().unwrap();
    let mut nbrs: Vec<(usize, usize)> = adj(cx, cy, w, h)
        .into_iter().filter(|&(nx, ny)| !blocked[nx][ny] && !visited[nx][ny]).collect();
    nbrs.sort_by_key(|&(nx, ny)| degree(nx, ny, blocked, visited, w, h));
    for (nx, ny) in nbrs {
        visited[nx][ny] = true;
        path.push((nx, ny));
        let remaining = n - path.len();
        if connected(blocked, visited, seen, gen, w, h, remaining) {
            if search(blocked, visited, seen, gen, path, w, h, n, cycle) { return true; }
        }
        path.pop();
        visited[nx][ny] = false;
    }
    false
}

fn degree(x: usize, y: usize, blocked: &[Vec<bool>], visited: &[Vec<bool>], w: usize, h: usize) -> usize {
    adj(x, y, w, h).iter().filter(|&&(nx, ny)| !blocked[nx][ny] && !visited[nx][ny]).count()
}

fn connected(blocked: &[Vec<bool>], visited: &[Vec<bool>], seen: &mut [Vec<u32>], gen: &mut u32, w: usize, h: usize, expect: usize) -> bool {
    if expect == 0 { return true; }
    *gen += 1;
    let g = *gen;
    let mut start = None;
    'outer: for x in 0..w {
        for y in 0..h {
            if !blocked[x][y] && !visited[x][y] { start = Some((x, y)); break 'outer; }
        }
    }
    let (sx, sy) = match start { Some(s) => s, None => return false };
    let mut queue = VecDeque::new();
    queue.push_back((sx, sy));
    seen[sx][sy] = g;
    let mut count = 1;
    while let Some((x, y)) = queue.pop_front() {
        for (nx, ny) in adj(x, y, w, h) {
            if !blocked[nx][ny] && !visited[nx][ny] && seen[nx][ny] != g {
                seen[nx][ny] = g;
                count += 1;
                if count == expect { return true; }
                queue.push_back((nx, ny));
            }
        }
    }
    count == expect
}

Test 6: Orbital TSP - Space Station Route Planning (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving an Orbital Travelling Salesman Problem in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY number of stations from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-5 stations (simple brute-force permutations feasible)
- **Medium**: 6-10 stations (requires heuristics, some optimization)
- **Large**: 15-20 stations (complex orbital mechanics, needs efficient algorithms)
- **Extreme**: 50-100 stations (very complex, requires advanced heuristics)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with 2 to 100 stations. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N mu
Line 2: start orbit: x y z vx vy vz
Lines 3..N+2: station orbits: x y z vx vy vz

**Output format (stdout):**
One line containing N integers: the visitation order of station indices (0..N-1).


**Starting position (epoch T=0):**
Position: (7197.9, 3733.4, -6152.3) km
Velocity: (4.567, -4.577, -1.601) km/s
Altitude: ~3807 km

**Physics constants:**
- Earth gravitational parameter μ = 398600.4418 km³/s²
- Earth radius = 6371 km

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number of stations
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 100 stations
3. **Accuracy**: Should provide reasonable delta-V estimates

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0

View chain of thought

Visit a series of space stations orbiting Earth using the least fuel possible, then return to the starting station. Unlike a normal route-planning problem, every target is moving at thousands of metres per second and the “cost” of travelling between two stations depends on orbital physics, not straight-line distance.

The AI must combine route optimisation with orbital mechanics. Subpasses add more stations and trickier orbit geometries. The baseline uses a simple nearest-neighbour heuristic with approximate fuel estimates.

CSC : error CS5001: Program

Orbital TSP - 2 stations

View Code (10709 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class OrbitalTSP
{
    const double MU = 398600.4418; // Earth gravitational parameter km³/s²
    const double EARTH_RADIUS = 6371.0;

    static void Main()
    {
        var lines = new List<string>();
        string line;
        while ((line = Console.ReadLine()) != null)
            lines.Add(line);

        var firstLine = lines[0].Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        int n = int.Parse(firstLine[0]);

        var startOrbit = ParseOrbit(lines[1]);
        var stations = new Orbit[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            stations[i] = ParseOrbit(lines[2 + i]);

        double[,] costMatrix = ComputeCostMatrix(startOrbit, stations);
        int[] route = SolveTSP(n, costMatrix);

        Console.WriteLine(string.Join(" ", route));
    }

    static Orbit ParseOrbit(string line)
    {
        var parts = line.Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        return new Orbit
        {
            X = double.Parse(parts[0]),
            Y = double.Parse(parts[1]),
            Z = double.Parse(parts[2]),
            VX = double.Parse(parts[3]),
            VY = double.Parse(parts[4]),
            VZ = double.Parse(parts[5])
        };
    }

    static double[,] ComputeCostMatrix(Orbit start, Orbit[] stations)
    {
        int n = stations.Length;
        double[,] cost = new double[n + 1, n + 1];

        var orbitalParams = new OrbitalParams[n + 1];
        orbitalParams[0] = ComputeOrbitalParams(start);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            orbitalParams[i + 1] = ComputeOrbitalParams(stations[i]);

        for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++)
        {
            for (int j = 0; j <= n; j++)
            {
                if (i == j)
                    cost[i, j] = 0;
                else
                    cost[i, j] = EstimateTransferDeltaV(orbitalParams[i], orbitalParams[j]);
            }
        }
        return cost;
    }

    static OrbitalParams ComputeOrbitalParams(Orbit o)
    {
        double r = Math.Sqrt(o.X * o.X + o.Y * o.Y + o.Z * o.Z);
        double v = Math.Sqrt(o.VX * o.VX + o.VY * o.VY + o.VZ * o.VZ);

        double energy = v * v / 2 - MU / r;
        double a = -MU / (2 * energy);

        double hx = o.Y * o.VZ - o.Z * o.VY;
        double hy = o.Z * o.VX - o.X * o.VZ;
        double hz = o.X * o.VY - o.Y * o.VX;
        double h = Math.Sqrt(hx * hx + hy * hy + hz * hz);

        double inc = Math.Acos(Math.Max(-1, Math.Min(1, hz / h)));

        double p = h * h / MU;
        double ecc = Math.Sqrt(Math.Max(0, 1 - p / a));

        double raan = 0;
        double nMag = Math.Sqrt(hx * hx + hy * hy);
        if (nMag > 1e-10)
        {
            raan = Math.Atan2(hx, -hy);
            if (raan < 0) raan += 2 * Math.PI;
        }

        return new OrbitalParams { A = a, E = ecc, Inc = inc, RAAN = raan, H = h };
    }

    static double EstimateTransferDeltaV(OrbitalParams from, OrbitalParams to)
    {
        if (from.A <= 0 || to.A <= 0) return 1e9;

        double r1 = from.A * (1 - from.E * from.E) / (1 + from.E);
        double r2 = to.A * (1 - to.E * to.E) / (1 + to.E);

        if (r1 <= EARTH_RADIUS || r2 <= EARTH_RADIUS) return 1e9;

        double v1 = Math.Sqrt(MU / r1);
        double v2 = Math.Sqrt(MU / r2);

        double aTransfer = (r1 + r2) / 2;
        double vTransfer1 = Math.Sqrt(MU * (2 / r1 - 1 / aTransfer));
        double vTransfer2 = Math.Sqrt(MU * (2 / r2 - 1 / aTransfer));

        double dv1 = Math.Abs(vTransfer1 - v1);
        double dv2 = Math.Abs(v2 - vTransfer2);
        double dvHohmann = dv1 + dv2;

        double incChange = Math.Abs(from.Inc - to.Inc);
        double raanChange = Math.Abs(from.RAAN - to.RAAN);
        if (raanChange > Math.PI) raanChange = 2 * Math.PI - raanChange;

        double planeChange = Math.Sqrt(incChange * incChange +
            Math.Pow(raanChange * Math.Sin((from.Inc + to.Inc) / 2), 2));

        double avgV = (v1 + v2) / 2;
        double dvPlane = 2 * avgV * Math.Sin(planeChange / 2);

        double eccPenalty = Math.Abs(from.E - to.E) * avgV * 0.5;

        return dvHohmann + dvPlane + eccPenalty;
    }

    static int[] SolveTSP(int n, double[,] cost)
    {
        if (n == 1) return new[] { 0 };
        if (n == 2) return cost[1, 2] + cost[2, 1] < cost[2, 1] + cost[1, 2] ? new[] { 0, 1 } : new[] { 0, 1 };

        if (n <= 10)
            return SolveTSPBruteForce(n, cost);
        else
            return SolveTSPHeuristic(n, cost);
    }

    static int[] SolveTSPBruteForce(int n, double[,] cost)
    {
        int[] best = null;
        double bestCost = double.MaxValue;

        var perm = Enumerable.Range(0, n).ToArray();
        do
        {
            double c = cost[0, perm[0] + 1];
            for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; i++)
                c += cost[perm[i] + 1, perm[i + 1] + 1];

            if (c < bestCost)
            {
                bestCost = c;
                best = (int[])perm.Clone();
            }
        } while (NextPermutation(perm));

        return best;
    }

    static bool NextPermutation(int[] arr)
    {
        int i = arr.Length - 2;
        while (i >= 0 && arr[i] >= arr[i + 1]) i--;
        if (i < 0) return false;

        int j = arr.Length - 1;
        while (arr[j] <= arr[i]) j--;

        (arr[i], arr[j]) = (arr[j], arr[i]);
        Array.Reverse(arr, i + 1, arr.Length - i - 1);
        return true;
    }

    static int[] SolveTSPHeuristic(int n, double[,] cost)
    {
        int[] route = NearestNeighbor(n, cost);
        route = TwoOpt(n, cost, route);

        if (n <= 50)
        {
            for (int iter = 0; iter < 5; iter++)
            {
                var newRoute = RandomNearestNeighbor(n, cost, iter);
                newRoute = TwoOpt(n, cost, newRoute);
                if (RouteCost(n, cost, newRoute) < RouteCost(n, cost, route))
                    route = newRoute;
            }
        }

        if (n >= 20)
            route = SimulatedAnnealing(n, cost, route);

        return route;
    }

    static int[] NearestNeighbor(int n, double[,] cost)
    {
        var route = new List<int>();
        var visited = new bool[n];
        int current = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            double minCost = double.MaxValue;
            int next = -1;

            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++)
            {
                if (!visited[j])
                {
                    double c = (i == 0) ? cost[0, j + 1] : cost[current + 1, j + 1];
                    if (c < minCost)
                    {
                        minCost = c;
                        next = j;
                    }
                }
            }

            route.Add(next);
            visited[next] = true;
            current = next;
        }

        return route.ToArray();
    }

    static int[] RandomNearestNeighbor(int n, double[,] cost, int seed)
    {
        var rng = new Random(seed * 12345);
        var route = new List<int>();
        var visited = new bool[n];
        int current = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            var candidates = new List<(int idx, double c)>();

            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++)
            {
                if (!visited[j])
                {
                    double c = (i == 0) ? cost[0, j + 1] : cost[current + 1, j + 1];
                    candidates.Add((j, c));
                }
            }

            candidates.Sort((a, b) => a.c.CompareTo(b.c));
            int pickRange = Math.Min(3, candidates.Count);
            int pick = rng.Next(pickRange);
            int next = candidates[pick].idx;

            route.Add(next);
            visited[next] = true;
            current = next;
        }

        return route.ToArray();
    }

    static int[] TwoOpt(int n, double[,] cost, int[] route)
    {
        var best = (int[])route.Clone();
        bool improved = true;

        while (improved)
        {
            improved = false;
            for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; i++)
            {
                for (int j = i + 2; j < n; j++)
                {
                    double delta = TwoOptDelta(n, cost, best, i, j);
                    if (delta < -1e-10)
                    {
                        Array.Reverse(best, i + 1, j - i);
                        improved = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        return best;
    }

    static double TwoOptDelta(int n, double[,] cost, int[] route, int i, int j)
    {
        int a = (i == 0) ? 0 : route[i - 1] + 1;
        int b = route[i] + 1;
        int c = route[j] + 1;
        int d = (j == n - 1) ? 0 : route[j + 1] + 1;

        if (i == 0) a = 0;

        double oldCost, newCost;

        if (i == 0)
        {
            oldCost = cost[0, b] + cost[c, d == 0 ? 0 : d];
            newCost = cost[0, c] + cost[b, d == 0 ? 0 : d];
        }
        else
        {
            oldCost = cost[a, b] + cost[c, d == 0 ? 0 : d];
            newCost = cost[a, c] + cost[b, d == 0 ? 0 : d];
        }

        return newCost - oldCost;
    }

    static double RouteCost(int n, double[,] cost, int[] route)
    {
        double c = cost[0, route[0] + 1];
        for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; i++)
            c += cost[route[i] + 1, route[i + 1] + 1];
        return c;
    }

    static int[] SimulatedAnnealing(int n, double[,] cost, int[] initial)
    {
        var rng = new Random(42);
        var current = (int[])initial.Clone();
        var best = (int[])initial.Clone();
        double currentCost = RouteCost(n, cost, current);
        double bestCost = currentCost;

        double temp = bestCost * 0.1;
        double cooling = 0.9995;
        int iterations = Math.Min(n * n * 1000, 500000);

        for (int iter = 0; iter < iterations; iter++)
        {
            int i = rng.Next(n);
            int j = rng.Next(n);
            if (i > j) (i, j) = (j, i);
            if (i == j || j - i < 2) continue;

            double delta = TwoOptDelta(n, cost, current, i, j);

            if (delta < 0 || rng.NextDouble() < Math.Exp(-delta / temp))
            {
                Array.Reverse(current, i + 1, j - i);
                currentCost += delta;

                if (currentCost < bestCost)
                {
                    bestCost = currentCost;
                    best = (int[])current.Clone();
                }
            }

            temp *= cooling;
        }

        return best;
    }

    struct Orbit
    {
        public double X, Y, Z, VX, VY, VZ;
    }

    struct OrbitalParams
    {
        public double A, E, Inc, RAAN, H;
    }
}

Test 7: CSG Union of Two Polyhedra (C++) Score: 0.00 / 12
Typical Prompt:
You are implementing a CSG (Constructive Solid Geometry) Union operation in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY mesh complexity from trivial (6 vertex unit cubes) to
ludicrous scale (millions of vertices). The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: VA FA VB FB (vertex/face counts for mesh A and B)
Next VA lines: x y z (mesh A vertices)
Next FA lines: a b c (mesh A faces, 0-indexed)
Next VB lines: x y z (mesh B vertices)
Next FB lines: a b c (mesh B faces)

You can assume that the input geometry is clean, manifold, watertight, and free of self-intersections.

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: V F (vertex and face counts for union mesh)
Next V lines: x y z
Next F lines: a b c (0-indexed triangle indices)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on mesh complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for very complex meshes
3. **Correctness**: Must output correct union geometry. Must be watertight, connected,
manifold, free of holes and self intersections..

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given two overlapping 3D shapes (triangle meshes), merge them into a single combined shape — like gluing two lumps of clay together and keeping only the outer surface. The output mesh must be watertight with no self-intersections.

The AI must correctly handle the tricky geometry where the two shapes overlap: splitting triangles, discarding interior faces, and stitching everything back together. Subpasses use increasingly complex shape pairs.

 0.0000
[overlapping_boxes] TOPOLOGY ISSUES: 18 boundary edges (mesh has holes, not watertight); inconsistent face winding (18 edges, 21%). Result vol: 14.33, Expected (MC): ~15.04, Inputs: 8.00 + 8.00, Time: 0.1s

CSG Union - overlapping_boxes

View Code (13683 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <array>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <map>
#include <set>
#include <iomanip>
#include <functional>

using namespace std;

const double EPS = 1e-12;
const double MERGE_EPS = 1e-10;

struct Vec3 {
    double x, y, z;
    Vec3() : x(0), y(0), z(0) {}
    Vec3(double x_, double y_, double z_) : x(x_), y(y_), z(z_) {}
    Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& v) const { return {x+v.x, y+v.y, z+v.z}; }
    Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& v) const { return {x-v.x, y-v.y, z-v.z}; }
    Vec3 operator*(double s) const { return {x*s, y*s, z*s}; }
    Vec3 operator/(double s) const { return {x/s, y/s, z/s}; }
    double dot(const Vec3& v) const { return x*v.x + y*v.y + z*v.z; }
    Vec3 cross(const Vec3& v) const { return {y*v.z-z*v.y, z*v.x-x*v.z, x*v.y-y*v.x}; }
    double len2() const { return x*x + y*y + z*z; }
    double len() const { return sqrt(len2()); }
    Vec3 norm() const { double l=len(); return l>EPS ? *this/l : Vec3(1,0,0); }
    bool eq(const Vec3& v) const { return fabs(x-v.x)<MERGE_EPS && fabs(y-v.y)<MERGE_EPS && fabs(z-v.z)<MERGE_EPS; }
};

struct AABB {
    Vec3 lo{1e30,1e30,1e30}, hi{-1e30,-1e30,-1e30};
    void add(const Vec3& p) {
        lo.x=min(lo.x,p.x); lo.y=min(lo.y,p.y); lo.z=min(lo.z,p.z);
        hi.x=max(hi.x,p.x); hi.y=max(hi.y,p.y); hi.z=max(hi.z,p.z);
    }
    bool hit(const AABB& b) const {
        return lo.x<=b.hi.x+EPS && hi.x>=b.lo.x-EPS &&
               lo.y<=b.hi.y+EPS && hi.y>=b.lo.y-EPS &&
               lo.z<=b.hi.z+EPS && hi.z>=b.lo.z-EPS;
    }
    Vec3 mid() const { return (lo+hi)*0.5; }
};

struct BNode { AABB box; int L=-1,R=-1,s=0,n=0; bool leaf() const { return n>0; } };

class Mesh {
public:
    vector<Vec3> V;
    vector<array<int,3>> T;
    vector<BNode> bvh;
    vector<int> idx;
    AABB bbox;

    void build() {
        if (T.empty()) return;
        bbox = AABB();
        for (auto& v : V) bbox.add(v);
        idx.resize(T.size());
        for (size_t i = 0; i < T.size(); i++) idx[i] = (int)i;
        bvh.reserve(T.size()*2);
        mkNode(0, (int)T.size());
    }

    AABB tbox(int i) const {
        AABB b; b.add(V[T[i][0]]); b.add(V[T[i][1]]); b.add(V[T[i][2]]); return b;
    }

    int mkNode(int s, int e) {
        int id = (int)bvh.size();
        bvh.push_back({});
        for (int i = s; i < e; i++) bvh[id].box.add(tbox(idx[i]).lo), bvh[id].box.add(tbox(idx[i]).hi);
        if (e-s <= 4) { bvh[id].s=s; bvh[id].n=e-s; return id; }
        Vec3 ext = bvh[id].box.hi - bvh[id].box.lo;
        int ax = ext.y>ext.x ? (ext.z>ext.y?2:1) : (ext.z>ext.x?2:0);
        int m = (s+e)/2;
        nth_element(idx.begin()+s, idx.begin()+m, idx.begin()+e, [&](int a, int b) {
            Vec3 ca=tbox(a).mid(), cb=tbox(b).mid();
            return (ax==0?ca.x:ax==1?ca.y:ca.z) < (ax==0?cb.x:ax==1?cb.y:cb.z);
        });
        bvh[id].L = mkNode(s, m);
        bvh[id].R = mkNode(m, e);
        return id;
    }
};

bool rayTri(const Vec3& o, const Vec3& d, const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, const Vec3& c, double& t) {
    Vec3 e1=b-a, e2=c-a, h=d.cross(e2);
    double det = e1.dot(h);
    if (fabs(det) < EPS) return false;
    double f = 1.0/det;
    Vec3 s = o - a;
    double u = f * s.dot(h);
    if (u < -EPS || u > 1+EPS) return false;
    Vec3 q = s.cross(e1);
    double v = f * d.dot(q);
    if (v < -EPS || u+v > 1+EPS) return false;
    t = f * e2.dot(q);
    return t > EPS;
}

int rayCnt(const Vec3& p, const Vec3& d, const Mesh& m, int ni) {
    if (ni < 0) return 0;
    auto& nd = m.bvh[ni];
    Vec3 inv(1.0/(fabs(d.x)>EPS?d.x:EPS), 1.0/(fabs(d.y)>EPS?d.y:EPS), 1.0/(fabs(d.z)>EPS?d.z:EPS));
    double tmin=-1e30, tmax=1e30;
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
        double lo=i==0?nd.box.lo.x:i==1?nd.box.lo.y:nd.box.lo.z;
        double hi=i==0?nd.box.hi.x:i==1?nd.box.hi.y:nd.box.hi.z;
        double pp=i==0?p.x:i==1?p.y:p.z;
        double iv=i==0?inv.x:i==1?inv.y:inv.z;
        double t1=(lo-pp)*iv, t2=(hi-pp)*iv;
        if (t1>t2) swap(t1,t2);
        tmin=max(tmin,t1); tmax=min(tmax,t2);
    }
    if (tmax<-EPS || tmin>tmax+EPS) return 0;
    if (nd.leaf()) {
        int c = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < nd.n; i++) {
            int fi = m.idx[nd.s+i];
            auto& tri = m.T[fi];
            double t;
            if (rayTri(p, d, m.V[tri[0]], m.V[tri[1]], m.V[tri[2]], t)) c++;
        }
        return c;
    }
    return rayCnt(p, d, m, nd.L) + rayCnt(p, d, m, nd.R);
}

bool inside(const Vec3& p, const Mesh& m) {
    if (m.bvh.empty()) return false;
    if (p.x<m.bbox.lo.x-EPS || p.x>m.bbox.hi.x+EPS ||
        p.y<m.bbox.lo.y-EPS || p.y>m.bbox.hi.y+EPS ||
        p.z<m.bbox.lo.z-EPS || p.z>m.bbox.hi.z+EPS) return false;
    Vec3 dirs[] = {{1,0.1371,0.0419}, {0.0321,1,0.0853}, {0.0617,0.0258,1}};
    int v = 0;
    for (auto& d : dirs) if (rayCnt(p, d.norm(), m, 0) % 2 == 1) v++;
    return v >= 2;
}

double planeDist(const Vec3& p, const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, const Vec3& c) {
    Vec3 n = (b-a).cross(c-a);
    double l = n.len();
    if (l < EPS) return 0;
    return n.dot(p-a) / l;
}

bool segPlane(const Vec3& p1, const Vec3& p2, const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, const Vec3& c, double& t, Vec3& pt) {
    double d1 = planeDist(p1, a, b, c);
    double d2 = planeDist(p2, a, b, c);
    if ((d1 > EPS && d2 > EPS) || (d1 < -EPS && d2 < -EPS)) return false;
    if (fabs(d1-d2) < EPS) return false;
    t = d1 / (d1 - d2);
    if (t < -EPS || t > 1+EPS) return false;
    pt = p1 + (p2-p1) * t;
    return true;
}

bool ptInTri(const Vec3& p, const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, const Vec3& c) {
    Vec3 n = (b-a).cross(c-a);
    double area = n.len();
    if (area < EPS) return false;
    Vec3 n1 = (b-a).cross(p-a);
    Vec3 n2 = (c-b).cross(p-b);
    Vec3 n3 = (a-c).cross(p-c);
    double d1 = n.dot(n1), d2 = n.dot(n2), d3 = n.dot(n3);
    return d1 >= -EPS*area && d2 >= -EPS*area && d3 >= -EPS*area;
}

struct Seg { Vec3 a, b; };

bool triTriSeg(const Vec3& a0, const Vec3& a1, const Vec3& a2,
               const Vec3& b0, const Vec3& b1, const Vec3& b2, Seg& seg) {
    vector<Vec3> pts;
    Vec3 av[] = {a0,a1,a2}, bv[] = {b0,b1,b2};
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
        double t; Vec3 pt;
        if (segPlane(av[i], av[(i+1)%3], b0, b1, b2, t, pt)) {
            if (ptInTri(pt, b0, b1, b2)) pts.push_back(pt);
        }
        if (segPlane(bv[i], bv[(i+1)%3], a0, a1, a2, t, pt)) {
            if (ptInTri(pt, a0, a1, a2)) pts.push_back(pt);
        }
    }
    if (pts.size() < 2) return false;
    seg.a = pts[0]; seg.b = pts[1];
    double maxD = (seg.b-seg.a).len2();
    for (size_t i = 0; i < pts.size(); i++) {
        for (size_t j = i+1; j < pts.size(); j++) {
            double d = (pts[i]-pts[j]).len2();
            if (d > maxD) { maxD = d; seg.a = pts[i]; seg.b = pts[j]; }
        }
    }
    return maxD > EPS*EPS;
}

void findAllSegs(const Mesh& A, const Mesh& B, map<int, vector<Seg>>& segsA, map<int, vector<Seg>>& segsB) {
    function<void(int, int)> check = [&](int ai, int bi) {
        if (ai < 0 || bi < 0) return;
        auto& na = A.bvh[ai], &nb = B.bvh[bi];
        if (!na.box.hit(nb.box)) return;
        if (na.leaf() && nb.leaf()) {
            for (int i = 0; i < na.n; i++) {
                int fi = A.idx[na.s+i];
                auto& ta = A.T[fi];
                for (int j = 0; j < nb.n; j++) {
                    int fj = B.idx[nb.s+j];
                    auto& tb = B.T[fj];
                    Seg seg;
                    if (triTriSeg(A.V[ta[0]], A.V[ta[1]], A.V[ta[2]],
                                  B.V[tb[0]], B.V[tb[1]], B.V[tb[2]], seg)) {
                        segsA[fi].push_back(seg);
                        segsB[fj].push_back(seg);
                    }
                }
            }
            return;
        }
        if (na.leaf()) { check(ai, B.bvh[bi].L); check(ai, B.bvh[bi].R); }
        else if (nb.leaf()) { check(A.bvh[ai].L, bi); check(A.bvh[ai].R, bi); }
        else {
            check(A.bvh[ai].L, B.bvh[bi].L);
            check(A.bvh[ai].L, B.bvh[bi].R);
            check(A.bvh[ai].R, B.bvh[bi].L);
            check(A.bvh[ai].R, B.bvh[bi].R);
        }
    };
    if (!A.bvh.empty() && !B.bvh.empty()) check(0, 0);
}

void clipTri(const Vec3& v0, const Vec3& v1, const Vec3& v2, const vector<Seg>& segs,
             const Mesh& other, vector<array<Vec3,3>>& out) {
    Vec3 n = (v1-v0).cross(v2-v0);
    double area = n.len();
    if (area < EPS) return;

    vector<Vec3> pts;
    pts.push_back(v0); pts.push_back(v1); pts.push_back(v2);
    for (auto& s : segs) {
        if (ptInTri(s.a, v0, v1, v2)) pts.push_back(s.a);
        if (ptInTri(s.b, v0, v1, v2)) pts.push_back(s.b);
    }

    vector<Vec3> unique;
    for (auto& p : pts) {
        bool found = false;
        for (auto& q : unique) if (p.eq(q)) { found = true; break; }
        if (!found) unique.push_back(p);
    }

    if (unique.size() <= 3) {
        Vec3 ctr = (v0+v1+v2)/3.0;
        Vec3 test = ctr + n.norm() * EPS * 1000;
        if (!inside(test, other)) out.push_back({v0, v1, v2});
        return;
    }

    int axis = 0;
    if (fabs(n.y) > fabs(n.x)) axis = 1;
    if (fabs(n.z) > fabs(axis==0?n.x:n.y)) axis = 2;

    auto proj = [axis](const Vec3& p) -> pair<double,double> {
        if (axis == 0) return {p.y, p.z};
        if (axis == 1) return {p.x, p.z};
        return {p.x, p.y};
    };

    Vec3 ctr(0,0,0);
    for (auto& p : unique) ctr = ctr + p;
    ctr = ctr / (double)unique.size();

    auto [cx, cy] = proj(ctr);
    sort(unique.begin(), unique.end(), [&](const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
        auto [ax, ay] = proj(a);
        auto [bx, by] = proj(b);
        return atan2(ay-cy, ax-cx) < atan2(by-cy, bx-cx);
    });

    for (size_t i = 1; i + 1 < unique.size(); i++) {
        Vec3 a = unique[0], b = unique[i], c = unique[i+1];
        Vec3 tn = (b-a).cross(c-a);
        if (tn.len2() < EPS*EPS) continue;
        if (tn.dot(n) < 0) swap(b, c);
        Vec3 tctr = (a+b+c)/3.0;
        Vec3 test = tctr + n.norm() * EPS * 1000;
        if (!inside(test, other)) out.push_back({a, b, c});
    }
}

struct VHash {
    size_t operator()(const Vec3& v) const {
        auto h=[](double d){return hash<long long>()((long long)(d/MERGE_EPS+(d>=0?0.5:-0.5)));};
        return h(v.x) ^ (h(v.y)*31) ^ (h(v.z)*997);
    }
};
struct VEq { bool operator()(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) const { return a.eq(b); } };

class CSG {
    vector<Vec3> outV;
    vector<array<int,3>> outT;
    unordered_map<Vec3, int, VHash, VEq> vmap;
public:
    int addV(const Vec3& v) {
        auto it = vmap.find(v);
        if (it != vmap.end()) return it->second;
        int id = (int)outV.size();
        outV.push_back(v);
        vmap[v] = id;
        return id;
    }

    void addT(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, const Vec3& c) {
        Vec3 n = (b-a).cross(c-a);
        if (n.len2() < EPS*EPS) return;
        int i0=addV(a), i1=addV(b), i2=addV(c);
        if (i0==i1 || i1==i2 || i2==i0) return;
        outT.push_back({i0,i1,i2});
    }

    void processMesh(const Mesh& src, const Mesh& other, const map<int, vector<Seg>>& fsegs) {
        for (int fi = 0; fi < (int)src.T.size(); fi++) {
            auto& t = src.T[fi];
            Vec3 v0 = src.V[t[0]], v1 = src.V[t[1]], v2 = src.V[t[2]];

            auto it = fsegs.find(fi);
            if (it != fsegs.end() && !it->second.empty()) {
                vector<array<Vec3,3>> sub;
                clipTri(v0, v1, v2, it->second, other, sub);
                for (auto& tri : sub) addT(tri[0], tri[1], tri[2]);
            } else {
                Vec3 ctr = (v0+v1+v2)/3.0;
                Vec3 n = (v1-v0).cross(v2-v0).norm();
                Vec3 test = ctr + n * EPS * 1000;
                if (!inside(test, other)) addT(v0, v1, v2);
            }
        }
    }

    void compute(Mesh& A, Mesh& B) {
        A.build();
        B.build();

        map<int, vector<Seg>> segsA, segsB;
        findAllSegs(A, B, segsA, segsB);

        if (segsA.empty() && segsB.empty()) {
            Vec3 sA = (A.V[A.T[0][0]] + A.V[A.T[0][1]] + A.V[A.T[0][2]]) / 3.0;
            Vec3 sB = (B.V[B.T[0][0]] + B.V[B.T[0][1]] + B.V[B.T[0][2]]) / 3.0;
            bool aInB = inside(sA, B);
            bool bInA = inside(sB, A);
            if (aInB) {
                for (auto& t : B.T) addT(B.V[t[0]], B.V[t[1]], B.V[t[2]]);
            } else if (bInA) {
                for (auto& t : A.T) addT(A.V[t[0]], A.V[t[1]], A.V[t[2]]);
            } else {
                for (auto& t : A.T) addT(A.V[t[0]], A.V[t[1]], A.V[t[2]]);
                for (auto& t : B.T) addT(B.V[t[0]], B.V[t[1]], B.V[t[2]]);
            }
            return;
        }

        processMesh(A, B, segsA);
        processMesh(B, A, segsB);
    }

    void output() {
        cout << outV.size() << " " << outT.size() << "\n";
        for (auto& v : outV) cout << v.x << " " << v.y << " " << v.z << "\n";
        for (auto& t : outT) cout << t[0] << " " << t[1] << " " << t[2] << "\n";
    }
};

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);
    cout << fixed << setprecision(9);

    int VA, FA, VB, FB;
    cin >> VA >> FA >> VB >> FB;

    Mesh A, B;
    A.V.resize(VA); A.T.resize(FA);
    B.V.resize(VB); B.T.resize(FB);

    for (int i = 0; i < VA; i++) cin >> A.V[i].x >> A.V[i].y >> A.V[i].z;
    for (int i = 0; i < FA; i++) cin >> A.T[i][0] >> A.T[i][1] >> A.T[i][2];
    for (int i = 0; i < VB; i++) cin >> B.V[i].x >> B.V[i].y >> B.V[i].z;
    for (int i = 0; i < FB; i++) cin >> B.T[i][0] >> B.T[i][1] >> B.T[i][2];

    CSG csg;
    csg.compute(A, B);
    csg.output();

    return 0;
}

Test 8: Maze Solver (Rust) Score: 14.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a maze solver in Rust that must handle ANY maze complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale.

Your program will be tested with mazes ranging from 5x5 to 50000x50000.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Maze format:**
- 'A' = Start position
- 'B' = Goal/End position
- '#' = Wall (cannot pass through)
- ' ' (space) = Open path (can walk through)

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: H W (height and width)
Next H lines: the maze row strings (each W characters)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: N (number of steps in path)
Next N lines: x y (0-indexed coordinates from top-left)
Path should include both start (A) and end (B) positions.
Movement is only up/down/left/right (no diagonals).
Output nothing (or 0) if no path exists.

**Constraints:**
- Path must be continuous (each step adjacent to previous)
- Path must not go through walls ('#')
- Path must start at 'A' and end at 'B'
- Path must not loop even if the maze allows it.
- If multiple solutions exist, return any one.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt

View chain of thought

Find the shortest path through a maze from a start cell to an end cell. The maze is a grid of walls and open passages, and the AI must navigate from one corner to the other using the fewest steps possible.

Subpasses increase the maze size dramatically, testing whether the AI's approach scales to very large mazes. The baseline finds the true shortest path using breadth-first search.

 1.0000
[5x5] Path found, Time: 0.0s - path length 5

Maze Solver - 5x5

View Code (2767 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write, BufWriter};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut stdin = stdin.lock();
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());

    let mut line = String::new();
    stdin.read_line(&mut line).unwrap();
    let mut dims = line.split_whitespace();
    let h: usize = dims.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let w: usize = dims.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut maze: Vec<u8> = vec![b'#'; h * w];
    let mut start: u32 = 0;
    let mut end: u32 = 0;

    for y in 0..h {
        line.clear();
        stdin.read_line(&mut line).unwrap();
        let bytes = line.as_bytes();
        for x in 0..w.min(bytes.len()) {
            let c = bytes[x];
            let idx = y * w + x;
            maze[idx] = c;
            if c == b'A' { start = idx as u32; }
            if c == b'B' { end = idx as u32; }
        }
    }

    let mut parent: Vec<u8> = vec![0; h * w];
    let mut queue: VecDeque<u32> = VecDeque::new();

    queue.push_back(start);
    parent[start as usize] = 5;

    let (w32, h32) = (w as u32, h as u32);

    while let Some(idx) = queue.pop_front() {
        if idx == end { break; }

        let x = idx % w32;
        let y = idx / w32;

        if x + 1 < w32 {
            let ni = (idx + 1) as usize;
            if parent[ni] == 0 && maze[ni] != b'#' {
                parent[ni] = 1;
                queue.push_back(idx + 1);
            }
        }
        if x > 0 {
            let ni = (idx - 1) as usize;
            if parent[ni] == 0 && maze[ni] != b'#' {
                parent[ni] = 2;
                queue.push_back(idx - 1);
            }
        }
        if y + 1 < h32 {
            let ni = (idx + w32) as usize;
            if parent[ni] == 0 && maze[ni] != b'#' {
                parent[ni] = 3;
                queue.push_back(idx + w32);
            }
        }
        if y > 0 {
            let ni = (idx - w32) as usize;
            if parent[ni] == 0 && maze[ni] != b'#' {
                parent[ni] = 4;
                queue.push_back(idx - w32);
            }
        }
    }

    if parent[end as usize] == 0 && start != end {
        writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
        return;
    }

    let mut path: Vec<u32> = Vec::new();
    let mut curr = end;
    loop {
        path.push(curr);
        if curr == start { break; }
        curr = match parent[curr as usize] {
            1 => curr - 1,
            2 => curr + 1,
            3 => curr - w32,
            4 => curr + w32,
            _ => unreachable!(),
        };
    }
    path.reverse();

    writeln!(out, "{}", path.len()).unwrap();
    for idx in path {
        writeln!(out, "{} {}", idx % w32, idx / w32).unwrap();
    }
}

Test 9: 1D Cutting Stock - Minimum Waste (C#) Score: 0.00 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 1D Cutting Stock Problem in C#.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N stock_length
Line 2: N cut lengths separated by spaces

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next num_stocks lines: space-separated cut indices assigned to that stock (0-indexed)

**Example:**
For cuts=[30, 40, 50] and stock_length=100:
Input:
3 100
30 40 50

Output:
2
0 1
2

**Constraints:**
- Each cut must come from a single stock piece (no gluing)
- Multiple cuts can come from the same stock piece if they fit
- No cut can exceed stock_length
- Must handle varying numbers of cuts efficiently. You can use
multiple threads, you can micro-optimise code, you can write clever algorithms,
whatever it takes to handle the problem and solve it within 5 minutes even if
there are millions of cuts.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given fixed-length stock material and a shopping list of smaller pieces to cut, use as few stock pieces as possible. Every requested piece must be cut from somewhere, but any leftover material is wasted — so packing the cuts tightly matters.

This is essentially a bin-packing problem and is NP-hard. Subpasses scale from a handful of cuts up to millions, forcing the AI to write code that is both correct and fast. The baseline uses a simple first-fit-decreasing heuristic.

 0.0000
[5 cuts, stock=100] Compilation failed:
Determining projects to restore...
Restored D:\CodingBenchmark\compile_cache\claude-opus-4-5\csharp\project_50809978859b4f7d\Program.csproj (in 84 ms).
CSC : error CS5001: Prog

Cutting Stock - 5 cuts, stock=100

View Code (2516 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var sr = new StreamReader(Console.OpenStandardInput(), Encoding.UTF8, false, 1 << 20);
        string[] tokens = sr.ReadToEnd().Split((char[])null, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);

        int p = 0;
        int n = int.Parse(tokens[p++]);
        int L = int.Parse(tokens[p++]);

        if (n == 0)
        {
            Console.WriteLine("0");
            return;
        }

        int[] cuts = new int[n];
        int[] orig = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            cuts[i] = int.Parse(tokens[p++]);
            orig[i] = i;
        }

        Array.Sort(cuts, orig);
        Array.Reverse(cuts);
        Array.Reverse(orig);

        var binRem = new List<int>();
        var assign = new int[n];
        var bins = new SortedSet<(int rem, int idx)>();

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            int c = cuts[i];
            int o = orig[i];

            var view = bins.GetViewBetween((c, int.MinValue), (L + 1, int.MaxValue));
            var it = view.GetEnumerator();

            if (it.MoveNext())
            {
                var best = it.Current;
                it.Dispose();
                bins.Remove(best);
                int newRem = best.rem - c;
                binRem[best.idx] = newRem;
                assign[o] = best.idx;
                if (newRem > 0) bins.Add((newRem, best.idx));
            }
            else
            {
                it.Dispose();
                int b = binRem.Count;
                binRem.Add(L - c);
                assign[o] = b;
                if (L - c > 0) bins.Add((L - c, b));
            }
        }

        int numBins = binRem.Count;
        var contents = new List<int>[numBins];
        for (int i = 0; i < numBins; i++) contents[i] = new List<int>();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) contents[assign[i]].Add(i);

        var sw = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput(), Encoding.UTF8, 1 << 20);
        sw.WriteLine(numBins);
        for (int i = 0; i < numBins; i++)
        {
            var list = contents[i];
            if (list.Count > 0)
            {
                sw.Write(list[0]);
                for (int j = 1; j < list.Count; j++)
                {
                    sw.Write(' ');
                    sw.Write(list[j]);
                }
            }
            sw.WriteLine();
        }
        sw.Flush();
    }
}
