Lunar Lander - 100x50 - simple landing

View Rust Code (5284 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write};
use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
use std::thread;
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

#[derive(Clone, Default)]
struct LanderState {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    vx: f64,
    vy: f64,
    angle: f64,
    angular_vel: f64,
    fuel: f64,
    updated: bool,
}

struct MapInfo {
    gravity: f64,
    max_thrust: f64,
    target_x: f64,
    target_y: f64,
}

fn parse_initial_map<R: BufRead>(reader: &mut R) -> io::Result<MapInfo> {
    let mut first_line = String::new();
    reader.read_line(&mut first_line)?;
    let parts: Vec<f64> = first_line
        .split_whitespace()
        .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
        .collect();

    let height = parts[1] as usize;
    let gravity = parts[2];
    let max_thrust = parts[3];

    let mut second_line = String::new();
    reader.read_line(&mut second_line)?;
    let parts2: Vec<f64> = second_line
        .split_whitespace()
        .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
        .collect();

    let target_x = parts2[2];
    let target_y = parts2[3];

    for _ in 0..height {
        let mut line = String::new();
        reader.read_line(&mut line)?;
    }

    Ok(MapInfo {
        gravity,
        max_thrust,
        target_x,
        target_y,
    })
}

fn normalize_angle(a: f64) -> f64 {
    let pi = std::f64::consts::PI;
    let mut result = a;
    while result > pi { result -= 2.0 * pi; }
    while result < -pi { result += 2.0 * pi; }
    result
}

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut handle = stdin.lock();

    let map_info = match parse_initial_map(&mut handle) {
        Ok(info) => info,
        Err(_) => return,
    };

    drop(handle);

    let gravity = map_info.gravity;
    let max_thrust = map_info.max_thrust;
    let target_x = map_info.target_x;
    let target_y = map_info.target_y;

    let state = Arc::new(Mutex::new(LanderState::default()));
    let state_clone = Arc::clone(&state);

    thread::spawn(move || {
        let stdin = io::stdin();
        let handle = stdin.lock();

        for line in handle.lines() {
            if let Ok(line) = line {
                let parts: Vec<f64> = line
                    .split_whitespace()
                    .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
                    .collect();

                if parts.len() >= 7 {
                    let mut s = state_clone.lock().unwrap();
                    s.x = parts[0];
                    s.y = parts[1];
                    s.vx = parts[2];
                    s.vy = parts[3];
                    s.angle = parts[4];
                    s.angular_vel = parts[5];
                    s.fuel = parts[6];
                    s.updated = true;
                }
            }
        }
    });

    let mut stdout = io::stdout();
    let control_interval = Duration::from_millis(20);

    loop {
        let start = Instant::now();

        let s = {
            let guard = state.lock().unwrap();
            guard.clone()
        };

        if !s.updated {
            let _ = writeln!(stdout, "0.0 0.0");
            let _ = stdout.flush();
            thread::sleep(control_interval);
            continue;
        }

        let dx = target_x - s.x;
        let dy = target_y - s.y;
        let dist = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
        let altitude = dy;

        let max_hvel = 8.0;
        let desired_vx = (dx * 0.4).clamp(-max_hvel, max_hvel);

        let safe_vy = if altitude > 100.0 {
            12.0
        } else if altitude > 50.0 {
            8.0
        } else if altitude > 20.0 {
            4.0
        } else if altitude > 10.0 {
            2.5
        } else if altitude > 3.0 {
            1.5
        } else {
            0.8
        };

        let desired_vy = if altitude > 0.0 {
            safe_vy.min(altitude * 0.3 + 0.5)
        } else {
            -1.0
        };

        let vx_err = desired_vx - s.vx;
        let vy_err = desired_vy - s.vy;

        let kp_h = 1.5;
        let kp_v = 2.5;

        let ax = vx_err * kp_h;
        let ay = vy_err * kp_v - gravity;

        let desired_angle = if ay.abs() < 0.001 && ax.abs() < 0.001 {
            0.0
        } else {
            ax.atan2(-ay)
        };

        let accel_mag = (ax * ax + ay * ay).sqrt();
        let base_thrust = if max_thrust > 0.0 {
            accel_mag / max_thrust
        } else {
            0.0
        };

        let angle_err = normalize_angle(desired_angle - s.angle);
        let kp_ang = 4.0;
        let kd_ang = 2.0;
        let turn = (angle_err * kp_ang - s.angular_vel * kd_ang).clamp(-1.0, 1.0);

        let thrust_scale = (1.0 - angle_err.abs() / std::f64::consts::PI).max(0.3);
        let thrust = (base_thrust * thrust_scale).clamp(0.0, 1.0);

        let speed = (s.vx * s.vx + s.vy * s.vy).sqrt();
        let near = dist < 2.0 && altitude.abs() < 1.5;

        let (final_thrust, final_turn) = if near && speed < 2.0 {
            (0.0, 0.0)
        } else if s.fuel <= 0.0 {
            (0.0, turn)
        } else {
            (thrust, turn)
        };

        let _ = writeln!(stdout, "{:.4} {:.4}", final_thrust, final_turn);
        let _ = stdout.flush();

        let elapsed = start.elapsed();
        if elapsed < control_interval {
            thread::sleep(control_interval - elapsed);
        }
    }
}

View Rust Code (5284 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write};
use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
use std::thread;
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

#[derive(Clone, Default)]
struct LanderState {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    vx: f64,
    vy: f64,
    angle: f64,
    angular_vel: f64,
    fuel: f64,
    updated: bool,
}

struct MapInfo {
    gravity: f64,
    max_thrust: f64,
    target_x: f64,
    target_y: f64,
}

fn parse_initial_map<R: BufRead>(reader: &mut R) -> io::Result<MapInfo> {
    let mut first_line = String::new();
    reader.read_line(&mut first_line)?;
    let parts: Vec<f64> = first_line
        .split_whitespace()
        .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
        .collect();

    let height = parts[1] as usize;
    let gravity = parts[2];
    let max_thrust = parts[3];

    let mut second_line = String::new();
    reader.read_line(&mut second_line)?;
    let parts2: Vec<f64> = second_line
        .split_whitespace()
        .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
        .collect();

    let target_x = parts2[2];
    let target_y = parts2[3];

    for _ in 0..height {
        let mut line = String::new();
        reader.read_line(&mut line)?;
    }

    Ok(MapInfo {
        gravity,
        max_thrust,
        target_x,
        target_y,
    })
}

fn normalize_angle(a: f64) -> f64 {
    let pi = std::f64::consts::PI;
    let mut result = a;
    while result > pi { result -= 2.0 * pi; }
    while result < -pi { result += 2.0 * pi; }
    result
}

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut handle = stdin.lock();

    let map_info = match parse_initial_map(&mut handle) {
        Ok(info) => info,
        Err(_) => return,
    };

    drop(handle);

    let gravity = map_info.gravity;
    let max_thrust = map_info.max_thrust;
    let target_x = map_info.target_x;
    let target_y = map_info.target_y;

    let state = Arc::new(Mutex::new(LanderState::default()));
    let state_clone = Arc::clone(&state);

    thread::spawn(move || {
        let stdin = io::stdin();
        let handle = stdin.lock();

        for line in handle.lines() {
            if let Ok(line) = line {
                let parts: Vec<f64> = line
                    .split_whitespace()
                    .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
                    .collect();

                if parts.len() >= 7 {
                    let mut s = state_clone.lock().unwrap();
                    s.x = parts[0];
                    s.y = parts[1];
                    s.vx = parts[2];
                    s.vy = parts[3];
                    s.angle = parts[4];
                    s.angular_vel = parts[5];
                    s.fuel = parts[6];
                    s.updated = true;
                }
            }
        }
    });

    let mut stdout = io::stdout();
    let control_interval = Duration::from_millis(20);

    loop {
        let start = Instant::now();

        let s = {
            let guard = state.lock().unwrap();
            guard.clone()
        };

        if !s.updated {
            let _ = writeln!(stdout, "0.0 0.0");
            let _ = stdout.flush();
            thread::sleep(control_interval);
            continue;
        }

        let dx = target_x - s.x;
        let dy = target_y - s.y;
        let dist = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
        let altitude = dy;

        let max_hvel = 8.0;
        let desired_vx = (dx * 0.4).clamp(-max_hvel, max_hvel);

        let safe_vy = if altitude > 100.0 {
            12.0
        } else if altitude > 50.0 {
            8.0
        } else if altitude > 20.0 {
            4.0
        } else if altitude > 10.0 {
            2.5
        } else if altitude > 3.0 {
            1.5
        } else {
            0.8
        };

        let desired_vy = if altitude > 0.0 {
            safe_vy.min(altitude * 0.3 + 0.5)
        } else {
            -1.0
        };

        let vx_err = desired_vx - s.vx;
        let vy_err = desired_vy - s.vy;

        let kp_h = 1.5;
        let kp_v = 2.5;

        let ax = vx_err * kp_h;
        let ay = vy_err * kp_v - gravity;

        let desired_angle = if ay.abs() < 0.001 && ax.abs() < 0.001 {
            0.0
        } else {
            ax.atan2(-ay)
        };

        let accel_mag = (ax * ax + ay * ay).sqrt();
        let base_thrust = if max_thrust > 0.0 {
            accel_mag / max_thrust
        } else {
            0.0
        };

        let angle_err = normalize_angle(desired_angle - s.angle);
        let kp_ang = 4.0;
        let kd_ang = 2.0;
        let turn = (angle_err * kp_ang - s.angular_vel * kd_ang).clamp(-1.0, 1.0);

        let thrust_scale = (1.0 - angle_err.abs() / std::f64::consts::PI).max(0.3);
        let thrust = (base_thrust * thrust_scale).clamp(0.0, 1.0);

        let speed = (s.vx * s.vx + s.vy * s.vy).sqrt();
        let near = dist < 2.0 && altitude.abs() < 1.5;

        let (final_thrust, final_turn) = if near && speed < 2.0 {
            (0.0, 0.0)
        } else if s.fuel <= 0.0 {
            (0.0, turn)
        } else {
            (thrust, turn)
        };

        let _ = writeln!(stdout, "{:.4} {:.4}", final_thrust, final_turn);
        let _ = stdout.flush();

        let elapsed = start.elapsed();
        if elapsed < control_interval {
            thread::sleep(control_interval - elapsed);
        }
    }
}

Skipped due to earlier zero score
Test 24: 3D Lunar Lander Game (C++) Score: 0.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play a 3D Lunar Lander game in real-time.

Your C++ controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from tiny (100m) to huge (100km).
The same code must work for all scales.

Control a lunar lander in full 3D space to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a terrain file path and continuous state updates via stdin, and must
output thrust/rotation commands to stdout in real-time.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time.
If your code doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the IO buffer fills up, the ship
will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes
that async code doesn't have. Multithreaded code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Coordinate System:**
- X: horizontal (East), Y: horizontal (North), Z: UP.
- Gravity acts in the -Z direction.
- All positions are in meters. Velocities in m/s. Angles in radians.

**Physics:**
- Gravity = 1.62 m/s² (lunar) in -Z direction.
- Thrust is along the lander's local "up" axis. When pitch=0 and yaw=0, thrust is
straight up (+Z). Tilting via pitch/yaw redirects thrust.
- Pitch: tilts the lander. +pitch tilts forward, redirecting thrust partially into -X.
Pitch is clamped to [-pi/2, pi/2].
- Yaw: rotates heading around Z.
- Roll: rolls around the thrust axis (cosmetic only, does not affect thrust direction).
- Rotation dynamics: commands apply angular acceleration (2 rad/s² per unit).
Angular rates are damped by 0.95 per tick.
- Fuel: 100% thrust burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

**Input format (stdin):**
Initial header (whitespace separated decimal numbers, except terrain path):
```
width depth height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y start_z target_x target_y target_z
terrain_file_path
STATE
```

The terrain_file_path is on its own line (the path may contain spaces).
Read the entire line with getline() or equivalent.

**Terrain file format (binary):**
The terrain file defines the 3D world as a binary file. It is a sequence of
uint16_t triplets (little-endian), one per (x, y) cell, stored in row-major order:

```cpp
for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
for (int y = 0; y < depth; y++) {
uint16_t a = read_uint16_le(); // ground rock top
uint16_t b = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock bottom
uint16_t c = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock top
}
}
```

For each column (x, y), the vertical structure is:
- z < a: SOLID ROCK (ground)
- a <= z < b: OPEN AIR
- b <= z < c: SOLID ROCK (floating terrain feature, overhang, arch, etc.)
- c <= z: OPEN AIR

If b == c, there is no floating rock in that column (just ground + open sky).
The file size is exactly width * depth * 6 bytes.

You have a startup grace period to parse this file before state updates begin.
The grace period is 5 seconds plus 10 seconds per gigabyte of terrain file.
For a 100x100 map this is ~5s. For a 100000x10000 map (~6GB) this is ~65s.
You may continue parsing in a background thread after the simulation begins,
but you MUST be reading stdin and writing stdout by then to avoid a crash.

Then continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds, whitespace separated):
```
x y z vx vy vz pitch yaw roll pitch_rate yaw_rate roll_rate fuel_remaining
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following at a frequency of at least 10hz:
```
thrust pitch_cmd yaw_cmd roll_cmd
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max_thrust)
- pitch_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (pitch angular acceleration command)
- yaw_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (yaw angular acceleration command)
- roll_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (roll angular acceleration command)

To be crystal clear: four decimal numbers separated by spaces, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (misses 2 consecutive updates), the
engine will cut thrust automatically. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

**Landing:**
- The target is at (target_x, target_y, target_z) which is just above the terrain.
- You must land within a small radius of the target with total speed < 3 m/s.
- Landing zone radius is approximately max(5, min(width,depth)*0.02) meters.

**Terrain collisions:**
- You collide if you enter any solid cell as defined by the terrain file.
- Out of bounds (x<0, x>=width, y<0, y>=depth, z<0, z>=height) is also a collision.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 3 m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into terrain or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed simulation time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Land a spacecraft on a 3D lunar surface with full attitude control. Unlike the 2D version, the lander can pitch, yaw, and roll, and the terrain is a detailed 3D heightmap with floating rock obstacles. The AI must parse a binary terrain file and navigate to a safe touchdown.

Subpasses vary the terrain complexity, starting conditions, and obstacle density. The AI must handle 6-degree-of-freedom physics and real-time control in three dimensions.

 0.0000
[100x100x50 - simple 3D landing] End: crashed, Distance: 105.0m, Time: 12.67s

3D Lunar Lander - 100x100x50 - simple 3D landing

View C++ Code (6749 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <fstream>
#include <sstream>
#include <string>
#include <cmath>
#include <thread>
#include <mutex>
#include <atomic>
#include <chrono>
#include <limits>

constexpr double PI = 3.14159265358979323846;

struct State {
    double x = 0, y = 0, z = 0;
    double vx = 0, vy = 0, vz = 0;
    double pitch = 0, yaw = 0, roll = 0;
    double pitch_rate = 0, yaw_rate = 0, roll_rate = 0;
    double fuel = 100;
    bool valid = false;
};

std::mutex g_mutex;
State g_state;
std::atomic<bool> g_running{true};

double g_gravity, g_max_thrust;
double g_target_x, g_target_y, g_target_z;
double g_width, g_depth;

inline double clamp_d(double v, double lo, double hi) {
    return v < lo ? lo : (v > hi ? hi : v);
}

inline double wrap_angle(double a) {
    while (a > PI) a -= 2.0 * PI;
    while (a < -PI) a += 2.0 * PI;
    return a;
}

void input_reader() {
    std::string line;
    while (g_running && std::getline(std::cin, line)) {
        if (line.empty()) continue;
        std::istringstream iss(line);
        State s;
        if (iss >> s.x >> s.y >> s.z >> s.vx >> s.vy >> s.vz
                >> s.pitch >> s.yaw >> s.roll
                >> s.pitch_rate >> s.yaw_rate >> s.roll_rate >> s.fuel) {
            s.valid = true;
            std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(g_mutex);
            g_state = s;
        }
    }
    g_running = false;
}

int main() {
    std::ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(nullptr);

    double height, fuel, start_x, start_y, start_z;
    std::cin >> g_width >> g_depth >> height >> g_gravity >> g_max_thrust >> fuel;
    std::cin >> start_x >> start_y >> start_z >> g_target_x >> g_target_y >> g_target_z;
    std::cin.ignore(std::numeric_limits<std::streamsize>::max(), '\n');

    std::string terrain_path;
    std::getline(std::cin, terrain_path);

    std::string state_marker;
    std::getline(std::cin, state_marker);

    {
        std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(g_mutex);
        g_state.x = start_x;
        g_state.y = start_y;
        g_state.z = start_z;
        g_state.fuel = fuel;
        g_state.valid = true;
    }

    std::thread terrain_thread([&terrain_path]() {
        std::ifstream file(terrain_path, std::ios::binary);
    });
    terrain_thread.detach();

    std::thread reader(input_reader);

    double hover_thrust = g_gravity / g_max_thrust;
    double map_scale = std::min(g_width, g_depth);
    double max_cruise_speed = clamp_d(map_scale * 0.12, 12.0, 250.0);

    while (g_running) {
        State s;
        {
            std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(g_mutex);
            s = g_state;
        }

        double thrust = hover_thrust;
        double pitch_cmd = 0, yaw_cmd = 0, roll_cmd = 0;

        if (s.valid) {
            double dx = g_target_x - s.x;
            double dy = g_target_y - s.y;
            double dz = g_target_z - s.z;

            double horiz_dist = std::sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy);
            double dist_3d = std::sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz);
            double speed = std::sqrt(s.vx*s.vx + s.vy*s.vy + s.vz*s.vz);
            double horiz_speed = std::sqrt(s.vx*s.vx + s.vy*s.vy);

            double max_decel = g_max_thrust * 0.55;
            double stopping_dist = speed * speed / (2.0 * max_decel) * 2.2 + 20.0;

            double speed_limit = max_cruise_speed;
            if (dist_3d < stopping_dist) {
                speed_limit = std::sqrt(2.0 * max_decel * dist_3d * 0.35);
            }
            if (dist_3d < 50.0) speed_limit = std::min(speed_limit, 8.0);
            if (dist_3d < 15.0) speed_limit = std::min(speed_limit, 4.0);
            if (dist_3d < 5.0) speed_limit = std::min(speed_limit, 2.0);
            speed_limit = std::max(speed_limit, 0.5);

            double desired_vx = 0, desired_vy = 0, desired_vz = 0;

            if (horiz_dist > 2.0) {
                double h_scale = std::min(speed_limit / horiz_dist, 0.6);
                desired_vx = dx * h_scale;
                desired_vy = dy * h_scale;

                double safe_alt = g_target_z + std::min(50.0, horiz_dist * 0.3);
                if (s.z < safe_alt) {
                    desired_vz = std::min(5.0, (safe_alt - s.z) * 0.3);
                } else {
                    desired_vz = -std::min(2.0, (s.z - safe_alt) * 0.2);
                }
            } else {
                if (s.z > g_target_z + 1.0) {
                    double descent_rate = std::min(2.0, (s.z - g_target_z) * 0.2);
                    desired_vz = -clamp_d(descent_rate, 0.3, 2.0);
                } else if (s.z < g_target_z - 0.5) {
                    desired_vz = std::min(1.0, (g_target_z - s.z) * 0.3);
                } else {
                    desired_vz = -0.3;
                }

                desired_vx = dx * 0.3;
                desired_vy = dy * 0.3;
            }

            double k_vel = 1.3;
            double ax = (desired_vx - s.vx) * k_vel;
            double ay = (desired_vy - s.vy) * k_vel;
            double az = (desired_vz - s.vz) * k_vel + g_gravity;

            double horiz_accel = std::sqrt(ax*ax + ay*ay);
            double total_accel = std::sqrt(ax*ax + ay*ay + az*az);

            double pitch_desired = 0, yaw_desired = s.yaw;

            if (az > 0.01) {
                thrust = clamp_d(total_accel / g_max_thrust, 0.0, 1.0);

                if (horiz_accel > 0.01) {
                    yaw_desired = std::atan2(-ay, -ax);
                    pitch_desired = std::atan2(horiz_accel, az);
                }
                pitch_desired = clamp_d(pitch_desired, -1.3, 1.3);
            } else {
                thrust = 0.0;
                pitch_desired = 0.0;
            }

            double k_p = 2.8, k_d = 1.4;
            double pitch_err = wrap_angle(pitch_desired - s.pitch);
            double yaw_err = wrap_angle(yaw_desired - s.yaw);

            pitch_cmd = k_p * pitch_err - k_d * s.pitch_rate;
            yaw_cmd = k_p * yaw_err - k_d * s.yaw_rate;
            roll_cmd = -2.0 * s.roll_rate - 0.5 * s.roll;

            if (dist_3d < 3.0 && speed < 4.0) {
                if (s.vz < -2.0) {
                    thrust = clamp_d(thrust + 0.15, 0.0, 1.0);
                }
                pitch_cmd = clamp_d(-s.pitch * 4.0 - s.pitch_rate * 2.0, -1.0, 1.0);
                yaw_cmd *= 0.5;
            }

            thrust = clamp_d(thrust, 0.0, 1.0);
            pitch_cmd = clamp_d(pitch_cmd, -1.0, 1.0);
            yaw_cmd = clamp_d(yaw_cmd, -1.0, 1.0);
            roll_cmd = clamp_d(roll_cmd, -1.0, 1.0);
        }

        std::cout << thrust << " " << pitch_cmd << " " << yaw_cmd << " " << roll_cmd << "\n";
        std::cout.flush();

        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(40));
    }

    reader.join();
    return 0;
}

 0.0000
[200x200x100 - short 3D hop] End: crashed, Distance: 187.2m, Time: 11.71s

3D Lunar Lander - 200x200x100 - short 3D hop

View C++ Code (6749 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <fstream>
#include <sstream>
#include <string>
#include <cmath>
#include <thread>
#include <mutex>
#include <atomic>
#include <chrono>
#include <limits>

constexpr double PI = 3.14159265358979323846;

struct State {
    double x = 0, y = 0, z = 0;
    double vx = 0, vy = 0, vz = 0;
    double pitch = 0, yaw = 0, roll = 0;
    double pitch_rate = 0, yaw_rate = 0, roll_rate = 0;
    double fuel = 100;
    bool valid = false;
};

std::mutex g_mutex;
State g_state;
std::atomic<bool> g_running{true};

double g_gravity, g_max_thrust;
double g_target_x, g_target_y, g_target_z;
double g_width, g_depth;

inline double clamp_d(double v, double lo, double hi) {
    return v < lo ? lo : (v > hi ? hi : v);
}

inline double wrap_angle(double a) {
    while (a > PI) a -= 2.0 * PI;
    while (a < -PI) a += 2.0 * PI;
    return a;
}

void input_reader() {
    std::string line;
    while (g_running && std::getline(std::cin, line)) {
        if (line.empty()) continue;
        std::istringstream iss(line);
        State s;
        if (iss >> s.x >> s.y >> s.z >> s.vx >> s.vy >> s.vz
                >> s.pitch >> s.yaw >> s.roll
                >> s.pitch_rate >> s.yaw_rate >> s.roll_rate >> s.fuel) {
            s.valid = true;
            std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(g_mutex);
            g_state = s;
        }
    }
    g_running = false;
}

int main() {
    std::ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(nullptr);

    double height, fuel, start_x, start_y, start_z;
    std::cin >> g_width >> g_depth >> height >> g_gravity >> g_max_thrust >> fuel;
    std::cin >> start_x >> start_y >> start_z >> g_target_x >> g_target_y >> g_target_z;
    std::cin.ignore(std::numeric_limits<std::streamsize>::max(), '\n');

    std::string terrain_path;
    std::getline(std::cin, terrain_path);

    std::string state_marker;
    std::getline(std::cin, state_marker);

    {
        std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(g_mutex);
        g_state.x = start_x;
        g_state.y = start_y;
        g_state.z = start_z;
        g_state.fuel = fuel;
        g_state.valid = true;
    }

    std::thread terrain_thread([&terrain_path]() {
        std::ifstream file(terrain_path, std::ios::binary);
    });
    terrain_thread.detach();

    std::thread reader(input_reader);

    double hover_thrust = g_gravity / g_max_thrust;
    double map_scale = std::min(g_width, g_depth);
    double max_cruise_speed = clamp_d(map_scale * 0.12, 12.0, 250.0);

    while (g_running) {
        State s;
        {
            std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(g_mutex);
            s = g_state;
        }

        double thrust = hover_thrust;
        double pitch_cmd = 0, yaw_cmd = 0, roll_cmd = 0;

        if (s.valid) {
            double dx = g_target_x - s.x;
            double dy = g_target_y - s.y;
            double dz = g_target_z - s.z;

            double horiz_dist = std::sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy);
            double dist_3d = std::sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz);
            double speed = std::sqrt(s.vx*s.vx + s.vy*s.vy + s.vz*s.vz);
            double horiz_speed = std::sqrt(s.vx*s.vx + s.vy*s.vy);

            double max_decel = g_max_thrust * 0.55;
            double stopping_dist = speed * speed / (2.0 * max_decel) * 2.2 + 20.0;

            double speed_limit = max_cruise_speed;
            if (dist_3d < stopping_dist) {
                speed_limit = std::sqrt(2.0 * max_decel * dist_3d * 0.35);
            }
            if (dist_3d < 50.0) speed_limit = std::min(speed_limit, 8.0);
            if (dist_3d < 15.0) speed_limit = std::min(speed_limit, 4.0);
            if (dist_3d < 5.0) speed_limit = std::min(speed_limit, 2.0);
            speed_limit = std::max(speed_limit, 0.5);

            double desired_vx = 0, desired_vy = 0, desired_vz = 0;

            if (horiz_dist > 2.0) {
                double h_scale = std::min(speed_limit / horiz_dist, 0.6);
                desired_vx = dx * h_scale;
                desired_vy = dy * h_scale;

                double safe_alt = g_target_z + std::min(50.0, horiz_dist * 0.3);
                if (s.z < safe_alt) {
                    desired_vz = std::min(5.0, (safe_alt - s.z) * 0.3);
                } else {
                    desired_vz = -std::min(2.0, (s.z - safe_alt) * 0.2);
                }
            } else {
                if (s.z > g_target_z + 1.0) {
                    double descent_rate = std::min(2.0, (s.z - g_target_z) * 0.2);
                    desired_vz = -clamp_d(descent_rate, 0.3, 2.0);
                } else if (s.z < g_target_z - 0.5) {
                    desired_vz = std::min(1.0, (g_target_z - s.z) * 0.3);
                } else {
                    desired_vz = -0.3;
                }

                desired_vx = dx * 0.3;
                desired_vy = dy * 0.3;
            }

            double k_vel = 1.3;
            double ax = (desired_vx - s.vx) * k_vel;
            double ay = (desired_vy - s.vy) * k_vel;
            double az = (desired_vz - s.vz) * k_vel + g_gravity;

            double horiz_accel = std::sqrt(ax*ax + ay*ay);
            double total_accel = std::sqrt(ax*ax + ay*ay + az*az);

            double pitch_desired = 0, yaw_desired = s.yaw;

            if (az > 0.01) {
                thrust = clamp_d(total_accel / g_max_thrust, 0.0, 1.0);

                if (horiz_accel > 0.01) {
                    yaw_desired = std::atan2(-ay, -ax);
                    pitch_desired = std::atan2(horiz_accel, az);
                }
                pitch_desired = clamp_d(pitch_desired, -1.3, 1.3);
            } else {
                thrust = 0.0;
                pitch_desired = 0.0;
            }

            double k_p = 2.8, k_d = 1.4;
            double pitch_err = wrap_angle(pitch_desired - s.pitch);
            double yaw_err = wrap_angle(yaw_desired - s.yaw);

            pitch_cmd = k_p * pitch_err - k_d * s.pitch_rate;
            yaw_cmd = k_p * yaw_err - k_d * s.yaw_rate;
            roll_cmd = -2.0 * s.roll_rate - 0.5 * s.roll;

            if (dist_3d < 3.0 && speed < 4.0) {
                if (s.vz < -2.0) {
                    thrust = clamp_d(thrust + 0.15, 0.0, 1.0);
                }
                pitch_cmd = clamp_d(-s.pitch * 4.0 - s.pitch_rate * 2.0, -1.0, 1.0);
                yaw_cmd *= 0.5;
            }

            thrust = clamp_d(thrust, 0.0, 1.0);
            pitch_cmd = clamp_d(pitch_cmd, -1.0, 1.0);
            yaw_cmd = clamp_d(yaw_cmd, -1.0, 1.0);
            roll_cmd = clamp_d(roll_cmd, -1.0, 1.0);
        }

        std::cout << thrust << " " << pitch_cmd << " " << yaw_cmd << " " << roll_cmd << "\n";
        std::cout.flush();

        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(40));
    }

    reader.join();
    return 0;
}
Session Stats
Ticks 67 Sim time 6.7s

Test 25: Asteroid Interception (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to calculate an asteroid interception trajectory.

You must write a C# solver that can handle an interception and redirection scenarios, from
short warning times (1-2 years), large impactors, generous delta-v, simple trajectories to
very long warning times (30-50 years), very small impactors, very tight delta-v,
advanced trajectory optimization with multi-flyby maneuvers and gravitational assist sequences required.

**Problem:**
Calculate a spacecraft trajectory to intercept and deflect an asteroid on collision course
with Earth using kinetic impact.

**The solver must output:**
1. Launch window and initial trajectory
2. Mid-course correction burn(s)
3. Final approach for kinetic impact

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal/scientific notation numbers):**
```
num_bodies warning_days impactor_mass delta_v_budget asteroid_mass t0_unix_utc
body_name mass x y z vx vy vz (for each body)
de421.bsp_file_path
```

All values are in SI units unless otherwise specified. Eg mass of earth is 5.972e24 kg.
All positions + velocities are relative to solar system barycenter.
Delta_v budget is in m/s.

The body name for the asteriod is "Asteroid". The body name for where your spacecraft originates
and you must redirect the asteroid to avoid is "Earth".

**Output format (stdout):**
```
num_burns
launch_time burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
correction_time1 burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
...
impact_time impact_dv_x impact_dv_y impact_dv_z
```

**Time units:**
- All times are in **days since scenario start (t=0 at the provided initial state)**.
- The **launch window is [0, warning_days]** (inclusive).
- **t0_unix_utc** is the UTC time of the provided initial state (t=0).

The simulation uses the JPL DE421 model of the solar system with Sun, Earth, Moon, and major planets,
and a KDK integrator to calculate trajectories. The path to the DE421 model is provided in the input
so your code can precisely match the simulation at extreme precision.

**Success Criteria:**
- Spacecraft intercepts asteroid before Earth impact (within 1000km)
- Kinetic impact transfers enough momentum to deflect asteroid sufficiently to miss Earth
- Total delta-v usage stays within budget
- Launch occurs within specified window

**Simulation host environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with C# .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable warning times and delta-v budgets
4. Complete analysis within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying mission complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Launch an interceptor spacecraft to collide with a dangerous asteroid before it reaches Earth. The AI must plan a trajectory through the solar system, accounting for the gravitational pull of the Sun and planets, to hit a moving target millions of kilometres away.

Harder subpasses give less warning time or tighter fuel budgets, potentially requiring planetary gravity assists (slingshots) to reach the asteroid. The AI must implement orbital mechanics and trajectory optimisation from scratch.

Asteroid Interception - 10 years, 10t impactor, 5km/s dV

View C# Code (15414 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Globalization;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;

class AsteroidInterceptor
{
    const double G = 6.67430e-11;
    const double AU = 1.495978707e11;
    const double DAY = 86400.0;
    const double EARTH_RADIUS = 6.371e6;
    const double SUN_MU = 1.32712440018e20;

    static void Main(string[] args)
    {
        CultureInfo.CurrentCulture = CultureInfo.InvariantCulture;

        var input = Console.In.ReadToEnd().Split((char[])null, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        int idx = 0;

        int numBodies = int.Parse(input[idx++]);
        double warningDays = double.Parse(input[idx++]);
        double impactorMass = double.Parse(input[idx++]);
        double deltaVBudget = double.Parse(input[idx++]);
        double asteroidMass = double.Parse(input[idx++]);
        double t0Unix = double.Parse(input[idx++]);

        var bodies = new List<Body>();
        Body asteroid = null, earth = null, sun = null;

        for (int i = 0; i < numBodies; i++)
        {
            string name = input[idx++];
            double mass = double.Parse(input[idx++]);
            double x = double.Parse(input[idx++]);
            double y = double.Parse(input[idx++]);
            double z = double.Parse(input[idx++]);
            double vx = double.Parse(input[idx++]);
            double vy = double.Parse(input[idx++]);
            double vz = double.Parse(input[idx++]);

            var body = new Body(name, mass, new Vec3(x, y, z), new Vec3(vx, vy, vz));
            bodies.Add(body);

            if (name == "Asteroid") asteroid = body;
            else if (name == "Earth") earth = body;
            else if (name == "Sun") sun = body;
        }

        string bspPath = input[idx++];

        double mu = sun != null ? G * sun.Mass : SUN_MU;

        var solution = FindInterceptTrajectory(bodies, asteroid, earth, sun, warningDays,
                                                deltaVBudget, asteroidMass, impactorMass, mu);

        Console.WriteLine(solution.Burns.Count);
        foreach (var burn in solution.Burns)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{burn.Time:G17} {burn.DV.X:G17} {burn.DV.Y:G17} {burn.DV.Z:G17}");
        }
    }

    static Solution FindInterceptTrajectory(List<Body> bodies, Body asteroid, Body earth,
                                            Body sun, double warningDays, double dvBudget,
                                            double asteroidMass, double impactorMass, double mu)
    {
        double maxT = warningDays * DAY;
        double earthImpactTime = EstimateImpactTime(asteroid, earth, bodies, maxT);
        double interceptDeadline = earthImpactTime * 0.95;

        Solution bestSolution = null;
        double bestScore = double.MaxValue;

        int launchSamples = Math.Min(60, (int)(warningDays / 4) + 15);
        int tofSamples = 40;

        for (int li = 0; li < launchSamples; li++)
        {
            double launchFrac = li / (double)(launchSamples - 1);
            double launchTime = launchFrac * Math.Min(warningDays * 0.6, interceptDeadline * 0.4) * DAY;

            var earthAtLaunch = PropagateBody(earth, bodies, launchTime);

            for (int ti = 0; ti < tofSamples; ti++)
            {
                double minTof = 20 * DAY;
                double maxTof = interceptDeadline - launchTime - 5 * DAY;
                if (maxTof < minTof) continue;

                double tofFrac = ti / (double)(tofSamples - 1);
                double tof = minTof + tofFrac * (maxTof - minTof);
                double arrivalTime = launchTime + tof;

                var asteroidAtArrival = PropagateBody(asteroid, bodies, arrivalTime);

                foreach (bool longWay in new[] { false, true })
                {
                    var lambert = SolveLambert(earthAtLaunch.Pos, asteroidAtArrival.Pos, tof, mu, longWay);
                    if (lambert == null) continue;

                    Vec3 launchDv = lambert.V1 - earthAtLaunch.Vel;
                    double launchDvMag = launchDv.Mag();

                    if (launchDvMag > dvBudget * 0.98) continue;

                    Vec3 relVel = lambert.V2 - asteroidAtArrival.Vel;
                    double impactSpeed = relVel.Mag();

                    double deflectionTime = earthImpactTime - arrivalTime;
                    if (deflectionTime < DAY) continue;

                    double momentumTransfer = impactorMass * impactSpeed * 1.5;
                    double dvAsteroid = momentumTransfer / asteroidMass;
                    double deflectionDist = dvAsteroid * deflectionTime;
                    double minSafeDist = EARTH_RADIUS * 5;

                    double deflectionScore = Math.Min(1.0, deflectionDist / minSafeDist);
                    double timeScore = (earthImpactTime - arrivalTime) / earthImpactTime;
                    double dvScore = launchDvMag / dvBudget;

                    double score = dvScore * 0.3 + (1 - deflectionScore) * 0.5 + (1 - timeScore) * 0.2;

                    if (score < bestScore && deflectionScore > 0.5)
                    {
                        bestScore = score;
                        bestSolution = new Solution();
                        bestSolution.Burns.Add(new Burn(launchTime / DAY, launchDv));

                        Vec3 impactDir = relVel.Normalized() * (-1);
                        double impactDvMag = Math.Min(impactSpeed, dvBudget - launchDvMag);
                        bestSolution.Burns.Add(new Burn(arrivalTime / DAY, impactDir * impactDvMag));
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        if (bestSolution == null)
        {
            bestSolution = CreateFallbackSolution(asteroid, earth, bodies, warningDays, dvBudget, mu, earthImpactTime);
        }

        return bestSolution ?? CreateMinimalSolution(warningDays);
    }

    static Solution CreateFallbackSolution(Body asteroid, Body earth, List<Body> bodies,
                                           double warningDays, double dvBudget, double mu, double impactTime)
    {
        double launchTime = Math.Min(10 * DAY, warningDays * 0.1 * DAY);
        double interceptTime = impactTime * 0.75;

        var earthAtLaunch = PropagateBody(earth, bodies, launchTime);
        var asteroidAtIntercept = PropagateBody(asteroid, bodies, interceptTime);

        double tof = interceptTime - launchTime;
        if (tof < DAY) return null;

        var lambert = SolveLambert(earthAtLaunch.Pos, asteroidAtIntercept.Pos, tof, mu, false);

        if (lambert != null)
        {
            Vec3 launchDv = lambert.V1 - earthAtLaunch.Vel;
            double launchDvMag = launchDv.Mag();

            if (launchDvMag > dvBudget)
            {
                launchDv = launchDv.Normalized() * (dvBudget * 0.95);
            }

            Vec3 relVel = lambert.V2 - asteroidAtIntercept.Vel;

            var solution = new Solution();
            solution.Burns.Add(new Burn(launchTime / DAY, launchDv));
            solution.Burns.Add(new Burn(interceptTime / DAY, relVel.Normalized() * Math.Min(relVel.Mag(), dvBudget * 0.04)));
            return solution;
        }

        return null;
    }

    static Solution CreateMinimalSolution(double warningDays)
    {
        var solution = new Solution();
        solution.Burns.Add(new Burn(1.0, new Vec3(5000, 0, 0)));
        solution.Burns.Add(new Burn(warningDays * 0.7, new Vec3(0, 100, 0)));
        return solution;
    }

    static double EstimateImpactTime(Body asteroid, Body earth, List<Body> bodies, double maxT)
    {
        double dt = DAY * 0.5;
        double minDist = double.MaxValue;
        double impactTime = maxT;

        var astState = new State(asteroid.Pos, asteroid.Vel);
        var earthState = new State(earth.Pos, earth.Vel);

        for (double t = 0; t <= maxT; t += dt)
        {
            if (t > 0)
            {
                astState = PropagateState(astState, asteroid, bodies, dt);
                earthState = PropagateState(earthState, earth, bodies, dt);
            }

            double dist = (astState.Pos - earthState.Pos).Mag();
            if (dist < minDist)
            {
                minDist = dist;
                impactTime = t;
            }

            if (dist < EARTH_RADIUS * 50 && dt > DAY * 0.1)
            {
                dt = DAY * 0.1;
            }
        }

        return impactTime;
    }

    static State PropagateBody(Body body, List<Body> allBodies, double time)
    {
        if (Math.Abs(time) < 1) return new State(body.Pos, body.Vel);

        double dt = Math.Min(3600, Math.Abs(time) / 100);
        int steps = (int)(Math.Abs(time) / dt);
        if (steps < 1) steps = 1;
        dt = time / steps;

        Vec3 pos = body.Pos;
        Vec3 vel = body.Vel;

        for (int i = 0; i < steps; i++)
        {
            Vec3 acc = ComputeAcceleration(pos, body, allBodies);
            vel = vel + acc * (dt * 0.5);
            pos = pos + vel * dt;
            acc = ComputeAcceleration(pos, body, allBodies);
            vel = vel + acc * (dt * 0.5);
        }

        return new State(pos, vel);
    }

    static State PropagateState(State state, Body self, List<Body> bodies, double dt)
    {
        Vec3 pos = state.Pos;
        Vec3 vel = state.Vel;

        Vec3 acc = ComputeAccelerationPos(pos, self, bodies);
        vel = vel + acc * (dt * 0.5);
        pos = pos + vel * dt;
        acc = ComputeAccelerationPos(pos, self, bodies);
        vel = vel + acc * (dt * 0.5);

        return new State(pos, vel);
    }

    static Vec3 ComputeAcceleration(Vec3 pos, Body self, List<Body> bodies)
    {
        Vec3 acc = new Vec3(0, 0, 0);

        foreach (var body in bodies)
        {
            if (body == self || body.Mass < 1e10) continue;

            Vec3 r = body.Pos - pos;
            double rMag = r.Mag();
            if (rMag < 1000) continue;

            double aMag = G * body.Mass / (rMag * rMag);
            acc = acc + r.Normalized() * aMag;
        }

        return acc;
    }

    static Vec3 ComputeAccelerationPos(Vec3 pos, Body self, List<Body> bodies)
    {
        return ComputeAcceleration(pos, self, bodies);
    }

    static LambertResult SolveLambert(Vec3 r1, Vec3 r2, double tof, double mu, bool longWay)
    {
        if (tof <= 0 || mu <= 0) return null;

        double r1Mag = r1.Mag();
        double r2Mag = r2.Mag();
        if (r1Mag < 1e6 || r2Mag < 1e6) return null;

        Vec3 c = r2 - r1;
        double cMag = c.Mag();
        if (cMag < 1e6) return null;

        double s = (r1Mag + r2Mag + cMag) / 2;
        double lambda2 = 1 - cMag / s;
        if (lambda2 < 0) lambda2 = 0;
        double lambda = Math.Sqrt(lambda2);

        Vec3 ir1 = r1.Normalized();
        Vec3 ir2 = r2.Normalized();
        Vec3 ih = ir1.Cross(ir2);
        double ihMag = ih.Mag();

        if (ihMag < 1e-10)
        {
            ih = new Vec3(0, 0, 1);
        }
        else
        {
            ih = ih.Normalized();
        }

        Vec3 it1, it2;
        if (ih.Z < 0)
        {
            lambda = -lambda;
            it1 = ir1.Cross(ih);
            it2 = ir2.Cross(ih);
        }
        else
        {
            it1 = ih.Cross(ir1);
            it2 = ih.Cross(ir2);
        }

        if (longWay)
        {
            lambda = -lambda;
            it1 = it1 * -1;
            it2 = it2 * -1;
        }

        double T = Math.Sqrt(2 * mu / (s * s * s)) * tof;

        double x = SolveLambertX(lambda, T);
        if (double.IsNaN(x) || double.IsInfinity(x)) return null;

        double y2 = 1 - lambda * lambda * (1 - x * x);
        if (y2 < 0) y2 = 0;
        double y = Math.Sqrt(y2);

        double gamma = Math.Sqrt(mu * s / 2);
        double rho = (r1Mag - r2Mag) / cMag;
        double sigma2 = 1 - rho * rho;
        if (sigma2 < 0) sigma2 = 0;
        double sigma = Math.Sqrt(sigma2);

        double lyx = lambda * y - x;
        double lypx = lambda * y + x;
        double ylx = y + lambda * x;

        double vr1 = gamma * (lyx - rho * lypx) / r1Mag;
        double vr2 = -gamma * (lyx + rho * lypx) / r2Mag;
        double vt1 = gamma * sigma * ylx / r1Mag;
        double vt2 = gamma * sigma * ylx / r2Mag;

        Vec3 v1 = ir1 * vr1 + it1 * vt1;
        Vec3 v2 = ir2 * vr2 + it2 * vt2;

        if (double.IsNaN(v1.Mag()) || double.IsNaN(v2.Mag())) return null;

        return new LambertResult(v1, v2);
    }

    static double SolveLambertX(double lambda, double T)
    {
        if (T <= 0) return double.NaN;

        double lambdaClamped = Math.Max(-0.99999, Math.Min(0.99999, lambda));

        double x = 0;
        double lower = -0.9999;
        double upper = 0.9999;

        for (int iter = 0; iter < 60; iter++)
        {
            double y2 = 1 - lambdaClamped * lambdaClamped * (1 - x * x);
            if (y2 < 0) y2 = 1e-10;
            double y = Math.Sqrt(y2);

            double Tx = ComputeTFromX(x, y, lambdaClamped);

            if (Math.Abs(Tx - T) < 1e-12) return x;

            if (Tx > T)
                lower = x;
            else
                upper = x;

            x = (lower + upper) / 2;
        }

        return x;
    }

    static double ComputeTFromX(double x, double y, double lambda)
    {
        double eta = x * x - 1;

        if (Math.Abs(eta) < 1e-10)
        {
            return 2.0 / 3.0 * (1 - lambda * lambda * lambda);
        }
        else if (eta < 0)
        {
            double S = Math.Sqrt(-eta);
            double psi = Math.Acos(x * y + lambda * (-eta));
            return (psi - lambda * S) / (S * S * S);
        }
        else
        {
            double S = Math.Sqrt(eta);
            double F = Math.Log(Math.Abs(y * S + x + lambda));
            return (lambda * S - F) / (S * S * S);
        }
    }

    class Vec3
    {
        public double X, Y, Z;
        public Vec3(double x, double y, double z) { X = x; Y = y; Z = z; }
        public double Mag() => Math.Sqrt(X * X + Y * Y + Z * Z);
        public Vec3 Normalized()
        {
            double m = Mag();
            return m > 1e-20 ? new Vec3(X / m, Y / m, Z / m) : new Vec3(0, 0, 0);
        }
        public static Vec3 operator +(Vec3 a, Vec3 b) => new Vec3(a.X + b.X, a.Y + b.Y, a.Z + b.Z);
        public static Vec3 operator -(Vec3 a, Vec3 b) => new Vec3(a.X - b.X, a.Y - b.Y, a.Z - b.Z);
        public static Vec3 operator *(Vec3 a, double s) => new Vec3(a.X * s, a.Y * s, a.Z * s);
        public Vec3 Cross(Vec3 b) => new Vec3(Y * b.Z - Z * b.Y, Z * b.X - X * b.Z, X * b.Y - Y * b.X);
        public double Dot(Vec3 b) => X * b.X + Y * b.Y + Z * b.Z;
    }

    class Body
    {
        public string Name;
        public double Mass;
        public Vec3 Pos, Vel;
        public Body(string name, double mass, Vec3 pos, Vec3 vel)
        {
            Name = name; Mass = mass; Pos = pos; Vel = vel;
        }
    }

    class State
    {
        public Vec3 Pos, Vel;
        public State(Vec3 pos, Vec3 vel) { Pos = pos; Vel = vel; }
    }

    class Burn
    {
        public double Time;
        public Vec3 DV;
        public Burn(double time, Vec3 dv) { Time = time; DV = dv; }
    }

    class Solution
    {
        public List<Burn> Burns = new List<Burn>();
    }

    class LambertResult
    {
        public Vec3 V1, V2;
        public LambertResult(Vec3 v1, Vec3 v2) { V1 = v1; V2 = v2; }
    }
}

Test 26: 3D Point Clustering (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to cluster 3D points into exactly N clusters.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY clustering scenario from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small datasets (1M points, 10 clusters), basic k-means, memory-friendly
- **Medium**: Moderate datasets (10M points, 100 clusters), optimized k-means, streaming
- **Large**: Large datasets (100M points, 1000 clusters), mini-batch k-means, memory constraints
- **Extreme**: Massive datasets (1B-10B points, 10000 clusters), streaming algorithms, very tight memory

**The Challenge:**
Your Rust clustering algorithm will be tested with datasets ranging from millions to billions of 3D points. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales while respecting memory constraints.

**Problem:**
Given millions to billions of 3D points, partition them into exactly N clusters minimizing within-cluster variance (k-means objective). Points are provided as binary float32 triples for efficiency.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_points num_clusters
[x y z] (as binary float32 triples, num_points total)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
[cluster_index] (as binary uint32, one per point)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on point count and cluster count
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive datasets
3. **Memory**: Must handle streaming input for datasets that don't fit in memory
4. **Quality**: Minimize within-cluster variance while producing exactly N clusters

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small datasets (≤10M points)**: Can use standard k-means with full data in memory
- **Medium datasets (10M-100M points)**: Mini-batch k-means, streaming updates
- **Large datasets (100M-1B points)**: Streaming k-means with limited memory buffers
- **Very Large datasets (>1B points)**: Very fast streaming, approximate clustering

**Key Techniques:**
- **Streaming algorithms**: Process points in batches without storing all data
- **Mini-batch k-means**: Update centroids incrementally
- **Memory management**: Use fixed-size buffers for large datasets
- **Binary I/O**: Efficient reading/writing of binary data
- **Convergence detection**: Monitor when clustering stabilizes

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect dataset size and choose appropriate clustering algorithm
- Use efficient binary I/O (std::io::BufReader/BufWriter)
- Implement streaming updates for large datasets
- For very large datasets, use approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty clusters, convergence issues
- Use fast random access for centroid updates

**Binary Format Details:**
- Input: Points as little-endian f32 triples (12 bytes per point)
- Output: Cluster indices as little-endian u32 (4 bytes per point)
- Total input size: num_points × 12 bytes
- Total output size: num_points × 4 bytes

**Memory Constraints:**
- Small datasets: Can load all points into memory
- Medium datasets: Use streaming with moderate buffers
- Large datasets: Streaming with minimal memory footprint
- Very Large datasets: Extremely memory-efficient streaming

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc (edition 2021)
2. Read binary input from stdin, write binary output to stdout
3. Handle variable point counts and cluster numbers
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently
6. Produce exactly N clusters

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on clustering complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a cloud of 3D points, group them into clusters so that points in the same cluster are close together and points in different clusters are far apart. Think of sorting a jar of mixed beads by colour when all you can see is their position.

The AI must decide how many clusters exist and assign every point to one. Subpasses increase the number of points and make the clusters harder to separate. Scoring is based on how well the AI’s clusters match the true groupings.

 0.0000
[10K points, 10 clusters] Wrong output count: 0 vs 10000

3D Point Clustering - 10K points, 10 clusters

View Rust Code (7799 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, BufReader, BufWriter, Read, Write};

struct Rng(u64);

impl Rng {
    fn new(seed: u64) -> Self { Rng(seed) }

    fn next(&mut self) -> u64 {
        self.0 = self.0.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1442695040888963407);
        self.0
    }

    fn usize(&mut self, max: usize) -> usize {
        if max == 0 { 0 } else { (self.next() as usize) % max }
    }

    fn f64(&mut self) -> f64 {
        (self.next() >> 11) as f64 / (1u64 << 53) as f64
    }
}

type Point = [f32; 3];

#[inline(always)]
fn read_point<R: Read>(r: &mut R) -> io::Result<Point> {
    let mut buf = [0u8; 12];
    r.read_exact(&mut buf)?;
    Ok([
        f32::from_le_bytes([buf[0], buf[1], buf[2], buf[3]]),
        f32::from_le_bytes([buf[4], buf[5], buf[6], buf[7]]),
        f32::from_le_bytes([buf[8], buf[9], buf[10], buf[11]]),
    ])
}

#[inline(always)]
fn dist_sq(a: &Point, b: &Point) -> f32 {
    let (dx, dy, dz) = (a[0] - b[0], a[1] - b[1], a[2] - b[2]);
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

#[inline(always)]
fn nearest(p: &Point, cs: &[Point]) -> usize {
    let mut best = 0;
    let mut best_d = f32::MAX;
    for (i, c) in cs.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = dist_sq(p, c);
        if d < best_d { best_d = d; best = i; }
    }
    best
}

fn kpp_init(pts: &[Point], k: usize, rng: &mut Rng) -> Vec<Point> {
    if pts.is_empty() || k == 0 { return vec![]; }
    let k = k.min(pts.len());

    let mut cs = vec![pts[rng.usize(pts.len())]];
    let mut d: Vec<f32> = pts.iter().map(|p| dist_sq(p, &cs[0])).collect();

    for _ in 1..k {
        let total: f64 = d.iter().map(|&x| x as f64).sum();
        if total <= 0.0 {
            cs.push(pts[rng.usize(pts.len())]);
        } else {
            let mut r = rng.f64() * total;
            let mut chosen = pts.len() - 1;
            for (i, &di) in d.iter().enumerate() {
                r -= di as f64;
                if r <= 0.0 { chosen = i; break; }
            }
            cs.push(pts[chosen]);
            let nc = cs.last().unwrap();
            for (i, p) in pts.iter().enumerate() {
                d[i] = d[i].min(dist_sq(p, nc));
            }
        }
    }
    cs
}

fn kmeans<R: Read, W: Write>(r: &mut R, w: &mut W, n: usize, k: usize, rng: &mut Rng) -> io::Result<()> {
    let mut pts = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    for _ in 0..n { pts.push(read_point(r)?); }

    let k = k.min(n);
    if k == 0 { return Ok(()); }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n as u32 { w.write_all(&i.to_le_bytes())?; }
        return Ok(());
    }

    let mut cs = kpp_init(&pts, k, rng);
    let mut asgn = vec![0usize; n];

    let max_iter = if n > 10_000_000 { 15 } else { 30 };

    for _ in 0..max_iter {
        let mut changed = false;
        for (i, p) in pts.iter().enumerate() {
            let c = nearest(p, &cs);
            if c != asgn[i] { changed = true; asgn[i] = c; }
        }
        if !changed { break; }

        let mut sums = vec![[0.0f64; 3]; k];
        let mut cnts = vec![0usize; k];
        for (i, p) in pts.iter().enumerate() {
            let c = asgn[i];
            sums[c][0] += p[0] as f64;
            sums[c][1] += p[1] as f64;
            sums[c][2] += p[2] as f64;
            cnts[c] += 1;
        }
        for (i, s) in sums.iter().enumerate() {
            if cnts[i] > 0 {
                let cnt = cnts[i] as f64;
                cs[i] = [(s[0]/cnt) as f32, (s[1]/cnt) as f32, (s[2]/cnt) as f32];
            } else {
                cs[i] = pts[rng.usize(n)];
            }
        }
    }

    for &a in &asgn { w.write_all(&(a as u32).to_le_bytes())?; }
    Ok(())
}

fn minibatch<R: Read, W: Write>(r: &mut R, w: &mut W, n: usize, k: usize, rng: &mut Rng) -> io::Result<()> {
    let mut pts = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    for _ in 0..n { pts.push(read_point(r)?); }

    let k = k.min(n);
    if k == 0 { return Ok(()); }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n as u32 { w.write_all(&i.to_le_bytes())?; }
        return Ok(());
    }

    let sample_size = (k * 50).min(500_000).min(n);
    let sample: Vec<Point> = (0..sample_size).map(|_| pts[rng.usize(n)]).collect();
    let mut cs = kpp_init(&sample, k, rng);

    let batch_size = 50_000.min(n);
    let epochs = 5.min(50_000_000 / n.max(1));

    for _ in 0..epochs {
        for batch_start in (0..n).step_by(batch_size) {
            let batch_end = (batch_start + batch_size).min(n);
            let mut sums = vec![[0.0f64; 3]; k];
            let mut cnts = vec![0usize; k];

            for i in batch_start..batch_end {
                let c = nearest(&pts[i], &cs);
                sums[c][0] += pts[i][0] as f64;
                sums[c][1] += pts[i][1] as f64;
                sums[c][2] += pts[i][2] as f64;
                cnts[c] += 1;
            }

            let lr = 1.0 / (1.0 + (batch_start / batch_size) as f64 * 0.1);
            for (i, s) in sums.iter().enumerate() {
                if cnts[i] > 0 {
                    let cnt = cnts[i] as f64;
                    let avg = [(s[0]/cnt) as f32, (s[1]/cnt) as f32, (s[2]/cnt) as f32];
                    cs[i][0] = (cs[i][0] as f64 * (1.0 - lr) + avg[0] as f64 * lr) as f32;
                    cs[i][1] = (cs[i][1] as f64 * (1.0 - lr) + avg[1] as f64 * lr) as f32;
                    cs[i][2] = (cs[i][2] as f64 * (1.0 - lr) + avg[2] as f64 * lr) as f32;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    for p in &pts { w.write_all(&(nearest(p, &cs) as u32).to_le_bytes())?; }
    Ok(())
}

fn streaming<R: Read, W: Write>(r: &mut R, w: &mut W, n: usize, k: usize, rng: &mut Rng) -> io::Result<()> {
    let k = k.min(n);
    if k == 0 { return Ok(()); }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n { let _ = read_point(r)?; w.write_all(&(i as u32).to_le_bytes())?; }
        return Ok(());
    }

    let warmup = (k * 10).min(200_000).min(n);
    let mut sample = Vec::with_capacity(warmup);
    for _ in 0..warmup { sample.push(read_point(r)?); }

    let mut cs = kpp_init(&sample, k, rng);

    for _ in 0..5 {
        let mut sums = vec![[0.0f64; 3]; k];
        let mut cnts = vec![0usize; k];
        for p in &sample {
            let c = nearest(p, &cs);
            sums[c][0] += p[0] as f64;
            sums[c][1] += p[1] as f64;
            sums[c][2] += p[2] as f64;
            cnts[c] += 1;
        }
        for (i, s) in sums.iter().enumerate() {
            if cnts[i] > 0 {
                let cnt = cnts[i] as f64;
                cs[i] = [(s[0]/cnt) as f32, (s[1]/cnt) as f32, (s[2]/cnt) as f32];
            }
        }
    }

    let mut cnts = vec![0usize; k];
    for p in &sample { cnts[nearest(p, &cs)] += 1; }

    for p in &sample { w.write_all(&(nearest(p, &cs) as u32).to_le_bytes())?; }
    drop(sample);

    for _ in warmup..n {
        let p = read_point(r)?;
        let c = nearest(&p, &cs);
        w.write_all(&(c as u32).to_le_bytes())?;

        cnts[c] += 1;
        let alpha = 1.0 / cnts[c] as f32;
        cs[c][0] += alpha * (p[0] - cs[c][0]);
        cs[c][1] += alpha * (p[1] - cs[c][1]);
        cs[c][2] += alpha * (p[2] - cs[c][2]);
    }
    Ok(())
}

fn main() -> io::Result<()> {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut r = BufReader::with_capacity(1 << 20, stdin.lock());
    let mut w = BufWriter::with_capacity(1 << 20, stdout.lock());

    let mut hdr = String::new();
    r.read_line(&mut hdr)?;
    let mut it = hdr.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let k: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut rng = Rng::new(42);

    if n <= 10_000_000 {
        kmeans(&mut r, &mut w, n, k, &mut rng)?;
    } else if n <= 200_000_000 {
        minibatch(&mut r, &mut w, n, k, &mut rng)?;
    } else {
        streaming(&mut r, &mut w, n, k, &mut rng)?;
    }

    w.flush()
}

View Rust Code (7799 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, BufReader, BufWriter, Read, Write};

struct Rng(u64);

impl Rng {
    fn new(seed: u64) -> Self { Rng(seed) }

    fn next(&mut self) -> u64 {
        self.0 = self.0.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1442695040888963407);
        self.0
    }

    fn usize(&mut self, max: usize) -> usize {
        if max == 0 { 0 } else { (self.next() as usize) % max }
    }

    fn f64(&mut self) -> f64 {
        (self.next() >> 11) as f64 / (1u64 << 53) as f64
    }
}

type Point = [f32; 3];

#[inline(always)]
fn read_point<R: Read>(r: &mut R) -> io::Result<Point> {
    let mut buf = [0u8; 12];
    r.read_exact(&mut buf)?;
    Ok([
        f32::from_le_bytes([buf[0], buf[1], buf[2], buf[3]]),
        f32::from_le_bytes([buf[4], buf[5], buf[6], buf[7]]),
        f32::from_le_bytes([buf[8], buf[9], buf[10], buf[11]]),
    ])
}

#[inline(always)]
fn dist_sq(a: &Point, b: &Point) -> f32 {
    let (dx, dy, dz) = (a[0] - b[0], a[1] - b[1], a[2] - b[2]);
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

#[inline(always)]
fn nearest(p: &Point, cs: &[Point]) -> usize {
    let mut best = 0;
    let mut best_d = f32::MAX;
    for (i, c) in cs.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = dist_sq(p, c);
        if d < best_d { best_d = d; best = i; }
    }
    best
}

fn kpp_init(pts: &[Point], k: usize, rng: &mut Rng) -> Vec<Point> {
    if pts.is_empty() || k == 0 { return vec![]; }
    let k = k.min(pts.len());

    let mut cs = vec![pts[rng.usize(pts.len())]];
    let mut d: Vec<f32> = pts.iter().map(|p| dist_sq(p, &cs[0])).collect();

    for _ in 1..k {
        let total: f64 = d.iter().map(|&x| x as f64).sum();
        if total <= 0.0 {
            cs.push(pts[rng.usize(pts.len())]);
        } else {
            let mut r = rng.f64() * total;
            let mut chosen = pts.len() - 1;
            for (i, &di) in d.iter().enumerate() {
                r -= di as f64;
                if r <= 0.0 { chosen = i; break; }
            }
            cs.push(pts[chosen]);
            let nc = cs.last().unwrap();
            for (i, p) in pts.iter().enumerate() {
                d[i] = d[i].min(dist_sq(p, nc));
            }
        }
    }
    cs
}

fn kmeans<R: Read, W: Write>(r: &mut R, w: &mut W, n: usize, k: usize, rng: &mut Rng) -> io::Result<()> {
    let mut pts = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    for _ in 0..n { pts.push(read_point(r)?); }

    let k = k.min(n);
    if k == 0 { return Ok(()); }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n as u32 { w.write_all(&i.to_le_bytes())?; }
        return Ok(());
    }

    let mut cs = kpp_init(&pts, k, rng);
    let mut asgn = vec![0usize; n];

    let max_iter = if n > 10_000_000 { 15 } else { 30 };

    for _ in 0..max_iter {
        let mut changed = false;
        for (i, p) in pts.iter().enumerate() {
            let c = nearest(p, &cs);
            if c != asgn[i] { changed = true; asgn[i] = c; }
        }
        if !changed { break; }

        let mut sums = vec![[0.0f64; 3]; k];
        let mut cnts = vec![0usize; k];
        for (i, p) in pts.iter().enumerate() {
            let c = asgn[i];
            sums[c][0] += p[0] as f64;
            sums[c][1] += p[1] as f64;
            sums[c][2] += p[2] as f64;
            cnts[c] += 1;
        }
        for (i, s) in sums.iter().enumerate() {
            if cnts[i] > 0 {
                let cnt = cnts[i] as f64;
                cs[i] = [(s[0]/cnt) as f32, (s[1]/cnt) as f32, (s[2]/cnt) as f32];
            } else {
                cs[i] = pts[rng.usize(n)];
            }
        }
    }

    for &a in &asgn { w.write_all(&(a as u32).to_le_bytes())?; }
    Ok(())
}

fn minibatch<R: Read, W: Write>(r: &mut R, w: &mut W, n: usize, k: usize, rng: &mut Rng) -> io::Result<()> {
    let mut pts = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    for _ in 0..n { pts.push(read_point(r)?); }

    let k = k.min(n);
    if k == 0 { return Ok(()); }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n as u32 { w.write_all(&i.to_le_bytes())?; }
        return Ok(());
    }

    let sample_size = (k * 50).min(500_000).min(n);
    let sample: Vec<Point> = (0..sample_size).map(|_| pts[rng.usize(n)]).collect();
    let mut cs = kpp_init(&sample, k, rng);

    let batch_size = 50_000.min(n);
    let epochs = 5.min(50_000_000 / n.max(1));

    for _ in 0..epochs {
        for batch_start in (0..n).step_by(batch_size) {
            let batch_end = (batch_start + batch_size).min(n);
            let mut sums = vec![[0.0f64; 3]; k];
            let mut cnts = vec![0usize; k];

            for i in batch_start..batch_end {
                let c = nearest(&pts[i], &cs);
                sums[c][0] += pts[i][0] as f64;
                sums[c][1] += pts[i][1] as f64;
                sums[c][2] += pts[i][2] as f64;
                cnts[c] += 1;
            }

            let lr = 1.0 / (1.0 + (batch_start / batch_size) as f64 * 0.1);
            for (i, s) in sums.iter().enumerate() {
                if cnts[i] > 0 {
                    let cnt = cnts[i] as f64;
                    let avg = [(s[0]/cnt) as f32, (s[1]/cnt) as f32, (s[2]/cnt) as f32];
                    cs[i][0] = (cs[i][0] as f64 * (1.0 - lr) + avg[0] as f64 * lr) as f32;
                    cs[i][1] = (cs[i][1] as f64 * (1.0 - lr) + avg[1] as f64 * lr) as f32;
                    cs[i][2] = (cs[i][2] as f64 * (1.0 - lr) + avg[2] as f64 * lr) as f32;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    for p in &pts { w.write_all(&(nearest(p, &cs) as u32).to_le_bytes())?; }
    Ok(())
}

fn streaming<R: Read, W: Write>(r: &mut R, w: &mut W, n: usize, k: usize, rng: &mut Rng) -> io::Result<()> {
    let k = k.min(n);
    if k == 0 { return Ok(()); }
    if k >= n {
        for i in 0..n { let _ = read_point(r)?; w.write_all(&(i as u32).to_le_bytes())?; }
        return Ok(());
    }

    let warmup = (k * 10).min(200_000).min(n);
    let mut sample = Vec::with_capacity(warmup);
    for _ in 0..warmup { sample.push(read_point(r)?); }

    let mut cs = kpp_init(&sample, k, rng);

    for _ in 0..5 {
        let mut sums = vec![[0.0f64; 3]; k];
        let mut cnts = vec![0usize; k];
        for p in &sample {
            let c = nearest(p, &cs);
            sums[c][0] += p[0] as f64;
            sums[c][1] += p[1] as f64;
            sums[c][2] += p[2] as f64;
            cnts[c] += 1;
        }
        for (i, s) in sums.iter().enumerate() {
            if cnts[i] > 0 {
                let cnt = cnts[i] as f64;
                cs[i] = [(s[0]/cnt) as f32, (s[1]/cnt) as f32, (s[2]/cnt) as f32];
            }
        }
    }

    let mut cnts = vec![0usize; k];
    for p in &sample { cnts[nearest(p, &cs)] += 1; }

    for p in &sample { w.write_all(&(nearest(p, &cs) as u32).to_le_bytes())?; }
    drop(sample);

    for _ in warmup..n {
        let p = read_point(r)?;
        let c = nearest(&p, &cs);
        w.write_all(&(c as u32).to_le_bytes())?;

        cnts[c] += 1;
        let alpha = 1.0 / cnts[c] as f32;
        cs[c][0] += alpha * (p[0] - cs[c][0]);
        cs[c][1] += alpha * (p[1] - cs[c][1]);
        cs[c][2] += alpha * (p[2] - cs[c][2]);
    }
    Ok(())
}

fn main() -> io::Result<()> {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut r = BufReader::with_capacity(1 << 20, stdin.lock());
    let mut w = BufWriter::with_capacity(1 << 20, stdout.lock());

    let mut hdr = String::new();
    r.read_line(&mut hdr)?;
    let mut it = hdr.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let k: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut rng = Rng::new(42);

    if n <= 10_000_000 {
        kmeans(&mut r, &mut w, n, k, &mut rng)?;
    } else if n <= 200_000_000 {
        minibatch(&mut r, &mut w, n, k, &mut rng)?;
    } else {
        streaming(&mut r, &mut w, n, k, &mut rng)?;
    }

    w.flush()
}

Test 27: Graph Coloring (C++) Score: 1.00 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve the Graph Coloring problem.

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ graph colorer will be tested with graphs ranging from simple 50 nodes
to massive million-node graphs requiring 60 colours.

**Problem:**
Color a graph with exactly k colours such that no two adjacent vertices share the same colour.
This is NP-Complete and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges num_colors
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
color_0 color_1 ... color_(n-1) (color assignment for each vertex)
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All vertices assigned valid colors (0 to k-1)
- No adjacent vertices share the same color
- All vertices are colored (complete coloring)

**Failure Criteria:**
- Cannot find valid coloring within time limit
- Invalid color assignment (adjacent vertices same color)
- Incomplete coloring

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with g++ or MSVC (C++17)
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable graph sizes and color counts
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying graph complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on graph complexity.

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Colour the nodes of a graph using as few colours as possible, with the rule that no two connected nodes may share the same colour. This is the same idea as colouring a map so that no neighbouring countries are the same colour.

Finding the minimum number of colours is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the graph size and connectivity. The AI’s colouring must be valid (no clashes) and use as few colours as possible.

 1.0000
[20 vertices, 4 colors] Valid coloring in 0.07s

Graph Coloring - 20 vertices, 4 colors

View C++ Code (5147 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>
#include <queue>
#include <chrono>
#include <random>
#include <tuple>

using namespace std;

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m, k;
    cin >> n >> m >> k;

    vector<vector<int>> adj(n);
    vector<int> color(n, -1);
    vector<int> deg(n, 0);

    for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
        int u, v;
        cin >> u >> v;
        adj[u].push_back(v);
        adj[v].push_back(u);
        deg[u]++;
        deg[v]++;
    }

    auto getAvailColor = [&](int v) -> int {
        long long used = 0;
        for (int u : adj[v]) {
            if (color[u] >= 0 && color[u] < 64) {
                used |= (1LL << color[u]);
            }
        }
        for (int c = 0; c < k; c++) {
            if (!(used & (1LL << c))) return c;
        }
        return -1;
    };

    auto dsatur = [&]() -> bool {
        fill(color.begin(), color.end(), -1);
        vector<int> sat(n, 0);
        vector<long long> mask(n, 0);

        priority_queue<tuple<int,int,int>> pq;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            pq.push(make_tuple(0, deg[i], i));
        }

        int cnt = 0;
        while (!pq.empty() && cnt < n) {
            int s, d, v;
            tie(s, d, v) = pq.top();
            pq.pop();

            if (color[v] >= 0 || s != sat[v]) continue;

            int c = getAvailColor(v);
            if (c < 0) return false;

            color[v] = c;
            cnt++;

            for (int u : adj[v]) {
                if (color[u] < 0 && !(mask[u] & (1LL << c))) {
                    mask[u] |= (1LL << c);
                    sat[u]++;
                    pq.push(make_tuple(sat[u], deg[u], u));
                }
            }
        }
        return cnt == n;
    };

    auto greedyDeg = [&]() -> bool {
        fill(color.begin(), color.end(), -1);
        vector<int> ord(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) ord[i] = i;
        sort(ord.begin(), ord.end(), [&](int a, int b) {
            return deg[a] > deg[b];
        });

        for (int v : ord) {
            int c = getAvailColor(v);
            if (c < 0) return false;
            color[v] = c;
        }
        return true;
    };

    auto localSearch = [&]() -> bool {
        mt19937 rng(42);
        auto start = chrono::steady_clock::now();

        for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
            if (color[v] < 0) color[v] = rng() % k;
        }

        vector<int> conflictCount(n, 0);
        for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
            for (int u : adj[v]) {
                if (color[v] == color[u]) conflictCount[v]++;
            }
        }

        vector<int> conflicting;
        for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
            if (conflictCount[v] > 0) conflicting.push_back(v);
        }

        for (int iter = 0; iter < 50000000 && !conflicting.empty(); iter++) {
            int idx = rng() % conflicting.size();
            int cv = conflicting[idx];

            if (conflictCount[cv] == 0) {
                conflicting[idx] = conflicting.back();
                conflicting.pop_back();
                continue;
            }

            int oldC = color[cv];
            int bc = oldC, bconf = conflictCount[cv];

            vector<int> colorConf(k, 0);
            for (int u : adj[cv]) {
                colorConf[color[u]]++;
            }

            for (int c = 0; c < k; c++) {
                if (colorConf[c] < bconf) {
                    bconf = colorConf[c];
                    bc = c;
                }
            }

            if (bc != oldC) {
                for (int u : adj[cv]) {
                    if (color[u] == oldC) {
                        conflictCount[cv]--;
                        conflictCount[u]--;
                    }
                    if (color[u] == bc) {
                        conflictCount[cv]++;
                        conflictCount[u]++;
                        if (conflictCount[u] == 1) {
                            conflicting.push_back(u);
                        }
                    }
                }
                color[cv] = bc;

                if (conflictCount[cv] == 0) {
                    conflicting[idx] = conflicting.back();
                    conflicting.pop_back();
                }
            }

            if (iter % 500000 == 0) {
                auto elapsed = chrono::steady_clock::now() - start;
                if (chrono::duration_cast<chrono::seconds>(elapsed).count() > 270) {
                    break;
                }

                conflicting.clear();
                for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
                    if (conflictCount[v] > 0) conflicting.push_back(v);
                }
            }
        }

        for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
            for (int u : adj[v]) {
                if (color[v] == color[u]) return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    };

    bool solved = dsatur() || greedyDeg() || localSearch();

    if (solved) {
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (i > 0) cout << ' ';
            cout << color[i];
        }
        cout << '\n';
    }

    return 0;
}

Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the Minimum Vertex Cover problem.

Your code may see graphs with up to 2M vertices and 15M edges.

**Problem:**
Find the minimum set of vertices such that every edge has at least one endpoint in the set.
This is NP-Hard and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
vertex_count
vertex_1 vertex_2 ... vertex_k (vertices in the cover, space-separated)
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All edges are covered by selected vertices
- Vertex set size is minimized (or near-minimized for large graphs)
- Output format is correct

**Failure Criteria:**
- Not all edges are covered
- Timeout without solution
- Invalid output format
